subject: I put my name on the line for you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TUBERVILLE FOR GOVERNOR, INC
body:
I'm taking heat because you let me down.
| [ ](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=896e3203a50548ff9ef012ab8d70d828&_e=B6mFXQQRonxgC4q_arXI3VwQge8huk_2caTEMFkS-uE2wkXoj4ObNP8CUKPlIjK-730TW0xr7sdtq93YPEJX8YnXdYU5Y6LenL3jy2uos6fut8p-lYFEi5LPMj9uMXdepcNT__kBdvCwPGSHzLo_vHwIyHtwMa4tJPFfZCiEXHvk6faA6RNjV6xlRijKA7UBXRa_LAfiJuPr8whGqEx6xALD8ZPAVh9qyrCsphg5tvleMrfOK_EZof7FnjBPxzdtsWmpMu8Yr1n0NvuJEmx7mC_j_SxMXdbcHrp1w4H45c6pLF6z2yDXRX_ous8uCraF9V37UMVDERbUAYQGt7T9vr6rDh_Fge0dZdwbxCBuqc7SbsfLnmCMl1UzAKCjJxRADPkNSnWlopb4Rn7Q5pMA9iwVe1DvquVzHEZjw57RDfLxNldbszems-Va7I9CiVx6PxmE6XZtZJ-QR9Ddaqc29VVtUQsZh0xDkLk9yX6wFgvMpBo2xbv3m6ib10XGUpsU6DgiLYL9Z0PTUu_-J6wcthjwaJyxcYQ07SpKkbuTDxqJX2uBTJmy7QJ71F7Yqekd)

Peter,

Kenny, VP of Membership Services here.

I need to be blunt with you because I don't have the luxury of time.

**I personally went out on a limb for you. I vouched for you. I put MY name on the line because I believed you 'd have our back.**

_But now? Because your GOP membership hasn 't been renewed, I'm taking heat. And frankly, I can't defend you much longer._

[**Only if you act ASAP can we fix this. Every minute that passes makes it harder for me to stand up for you.**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=896e3203a50548ff9ef012ab8d70d828&_e=B6mFXQQRonxgC4q_arXI3VwQge8huk_2caTEMFkS-uE2wkXoj4ObNP8CUKPlIjK-730TW0xr7sdtq93YPEJX8YnXdYU5Y6LenL3jy2uos6fut8p-lYFEi5LPMj9uMXdepcNT__kBdvCwPGSHzLo_vHwIyHtwMa4tJPFfZCiEXHvk6faA6RNjV6xlRijKA7UBe8S9MQDiZGJIPi8svLGjWFaQL6VtV1TmQjIr1rAMWCA-pF7mqYJZM730LZghJbmYNm9VlblhRYwuZwmz_2W9eB6QMYnt5KAJhPs9tyYi0--uS3mfrD7yJep3mLR1b-tlpO3zFh1KQhhBIT7utlGW0YjwJ5DvC84ydGp127WB-WRrzxAMVzK7EG0nZxNard_m6UXRShUg65qNzf3Hlu9ng5ANpNjfSheT_bQgP-ilm0v5o8cvXTIeAPuvxS_x3s53LsfSjoX1pq5SLrzik-upioBzA7jnUioBnET8J-tfFzHP4Ib2QYgV3h6tmP2v1inTRq7dT1Tw-BljtJY1b7_g8dFvC9Q7e8-Vahiv6rX7MLD0umt_CXaUB75O4EJa-acH)

Please, don't let me down. Don't leave me hanging. I need you to step up, [**renew your membership right now,**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=896e3203a50548ff9ef012ab8d70d828&_e=B6mFXQQRonxgC4q_arXI3VwQge8huk_2caTEMFkS-uE2wkXoj4ObNP8CUKPlIjK-730TW0xr7sdtq93YPEJX8YnXdYU5Y6LenL3jy2uos6fut8p-lYFEi5LPMj9uMXdepcNT__kBdvCwPGSHzLo_vHwIyHtwMa4tJPFfZCiEXHvk6faA6RNjV6xlRijKA7UB07moFwQ0zohPUoQr-tm2l9zyXqF3gm5M-J_ajeoo48EWgg88H-GHO8FG683ykpcS2H_Mk_TBpPtrpPwdp5V3BX3a5Fx3dCJXkKVSLh2jsMgArBg3VqdI4L6Gv70iJxlkfkDsLa-lIJbfsjtkmhFxHfwDYVusPZ-TtD4L9tCY8y9h3qdMpsU5-fVejHjPqOWU6OWg8HBvbRluWc0h3F1gNgIYh_0KIMoScmWePMGyutuzfuwXy14cTpkSWv9uL4F_mMykFQkPMukYQTgtGEjQvyleeLTt7ujvEAQiZqCLAOY7GoUOljikClmoW6Z2I17aMsbpH9vlMIrR-0RnUIWSEY82Snwr7tPaIK40QnzdcLFW7mWG1cqwQRcKd57Ybkl6) and show everyone that my trust in you wasn't misplaced.

name: Voter Action Project
subject: So much more at stake than one case
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
The last two decades of U.S. Supreme Court rulings and the written opinions of the six members of the suppressive majority tell us all we need to know about where these voting rights cases are going.
That's exactly why we need to fight back by putting defenders of voting rights in office to pass new laws that expand on and protect the VRA, alongside confirming judges who will defend our democracy and fundamental right to vote.
Informing and turning out voters uses resources we only get with your support. RUSH $25 or more right now to stand against voter suppression and usher in the pro-democracy movement we deserve >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=082125_3&amount=25]
Voter Action Project/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Slate: The Supreme Court Is Determined to Turn Voting Into a Limited Privilege [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=082125_3&amount=25]
At Voter Action Project, Team, we believe that voting is a fundamental right , not a privilege or some test of everyone's endurance and ability to wait in a long line. We believe that our right to vote is the core bedrock that makes up our democracy and needs to be defended, expanded, and celebrated.
However, as the Voting Rights Act -- a historic piece of legislation that stood for voting as a fundamental right for every American -- recently turned 60 years old, it is being eviscerated to stop voters and their lawyers from defending that freedom to vote in court.
Conservatives on the United States Supreme Court have spent the last two decades slowly tearing the VRA to pieces. In the hands of opponents of voting rights, the Voting Rights Act has faced death by a thousand cuts. Some of those cuts have been small, such as limiting how courts consider challenges brought under the law. And some have been large, as in the 2013 decision in Shelby County v. Holder that ended the requirement for certain states with histories of discrimination to submit election changes and district maps to the Department of Justice for approval.
As the Supreme Court holds over another case, hell-bent on dismantling the Voting Rights Act piece by piece, we have to fight to protect our fundamental rights.
Pitch in $25 or whatever you can today to stand up for the VRA and help us protect voting rights nationwide >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=082125_3&amount=25]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=082125_3&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=082125_3&amount=25] GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=082125_3&amount=50]
GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=082125_3&amount=100] GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=082125_3&amount=250] OTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=082125_3&amount=35]
Doubt us? The U.S. Supreme Court is now set to hear oral argument in Callais v. Landry — the high-stakes Louisiana redistricting case that could upend the Voting Rights Act — on October 15.
This will be the second time the nation’s highest court hears oral argument in the pivotal voting rights case. This fall, the argument will focus on whether Louisiana’s creation of a second majority-minorty district under the VRA violates the U.S. Constitution.
This is a key opportunity for Chief Justice John Roberts, who has taken every opportunity since being appointed to the court by President George W. Bush to destroy the VRA. This case could take out one of the last legs holding the law up in favor of equality and our fundamental right to have our voice heard in an election.
Over the last two decades, as the Supreme Court dismantled the VRA case by case, voter suppression has evolved. It’s been built on paperwork, confusion, inconvenience, and ever-changing rules. That’s not how it should be. We need to put justice back in reach for those who need it most.
Please, fuel our work to stop them with $25 or whatever you can today and defend our fundamental right to vote >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=082125_3&amount=25]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=082125_3&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=082125_3&amount=20]

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
subject: Not just anti-Trump
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
We need a positive, affirmative vision for this country.
#
I have a theory of power that is about **outside-inside power**.
Which means constantly building power and modes of accountability _inside_ government, and in the streets organizing the masses _outside_ government — in everyday, real life.
Right now, the inside power, Democrats are in the minority.
People don't understand that when we talk about midterm elections — who controls the House and who controls the Senate — it doesn't _just_ mean that there are more Republicans than Democrats.
It also means that Republicans control every committee.
They control everything that comes to the floor.
They control what legislation gets voted on or doesn't get voted on.
They control whether investigations happen or not.
Right now, Republicans are in control of the House, the Senate, and the White House, which means that Democrats do not have control of committees, investigations, or ability to conduct oversight in the congressional sense.
Now, where I have a departure with some others is that I'm a big believer in building outside power, outside organizing, etc.
And to me, **that means getting creative as a party**.
For example, we don't need Republicans to do hearings — Democrats can call shadow hearings where all Democrats invite a witness, talk about this, etc.
I did this last year with Jamie Raskin on the Oversight Committee. We conducted a shadow hearing around the corruption in the Supreme Court, which led to my filing articles of impeachment on Justice Alito.
Engaging in that kind of activity is important, and **continuing to build mass movements**.
These rallies that Bernie and I have been doing have been enormously powerful, not just from, “Let’s all get together and deliver a speech.” There is a lot of organizing infrastructure down ballot, and in community organizing non-electorally, that gets built when we select a location strategically for 20,000-30,000 people.
So it involves a lot of different pieces, building power, building accountability — and also building pressure inside Congress, including inside the Democratic Party.
One thing that I do think our party needs is **a vision that's not _just_ anti-Trump**.
This is a position I've long held: We actually need a positive, affirmative vision for this country, and be willing to take risks, and vote on real things, and _actually tell people what we're fighting for._
A lot of people don't know what an average Democrat fights for, because we need to build that consensus. But I don't think that we're starting with nothing.
This is where we have an opportunity to fight for minimum wage increases, to fight for guaranteed health care, to fight for something that's actually worthwhile.
Like in New York City: in a near historic heat wave, people stood in line in the hot sun, no air conditioning to vote for Zohran.
**You need to have a vision that's worth standing in the hot sun for hours to vote for**. People are not going to do that with only ‘being upset.’
It's important for us to hold the corruption of this administration accountable, but we also have to have an alternative vision.
And it can't just be, “Not that.” If it's _not that_ , then _what_ is it?
For me, my “what” is **raising the federal minimum wage to a living wage.**
My “what” is **guaranteed healthcare for every American** and catching up with the rest of the modern world.
My “what” is **making public colleges and universities tuition free.**
My “what” is actually having a **real path to citizenship** in the United States.
That’s why this movement is so important: we’re fighting for each and every one of these issues, from universal healthcare to getting money out of our politics.
[ Help us continue the fight with a grassroots contribution today.](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250821.ufmnnd&refcode=em20250821-9597-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9597%2E2526796%2EBH_i0x)
**[Contribute $5](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/14659?c0=aoc-email-20250821.ufmnnd&refcode=em20250821-9597-P_FND_EGR_GRM&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=20&akid=9597%2E2526796%2EBH_i0x)**
In solidarity,
Alexandria

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/aoc-email-20250821?refcode=em20250821-9597-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=21&refcode2=9597_2526796_BH_i0x&akid=9597%2E2526796%2EBH_i0x)** | **[Merch](https://shop.ocasiocortez.com/?utm_source=em-footer-20250821-9597-P_FND_EGR_GRM&t=22&akid=9597%2E2526796%2EBH_i0x)**

* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=27&akid=9597%2E2526796%2EBH_i0x)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=9597%2E2526796%2EBH_i0x)

Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
clean_body: We need a positive, affirmative vision for this country. I have a theory of power that is about outside-inside power. Which means constantly building power and modes of accountability inside government, and in the streets organizing the masses outside government — in everyday, real life. Right now, the inside power, Democrats are in the minority. People don't understand that when we talk about midterm elections — who controls the House and who controls the Senate — it doesn't just mean that there are more Republicans than Democrats. It also means that Republicans control every committee. They control everything that comes to the floor. They control what legislation gets voted on or doesn't get voted on. They control whether investigations happen or not. Right now, Republicans are in control of the House, the Senate, and the White House, which means that Democrats do not have control of committees, investigations, or ability to conduct oversight in the congressional sense. Now, where I have a departure with some others is that I'm a big believer in building outside power, outside organizing, etc. And to me, that means getting creative as a party. For example, we don't need Republicans to do hearings — Democrats can call shadow hearings where all Democrats invite a witness, talk about this, etc. I did this last year with Jamie Raskin on the Oversight Committee. We conducted a shadow hearing around the corruption in the Supreme Court, which led to my filing articles of impeachment on Justice Alito. Engaging in that kind of activity is important, and continuing to build mass movements. These rallies that Bernie and I have been doing have been enormously powerful, not just from, “Let’s all get together and deliver a speech.” There is a lot of organizing infrastructure down ballot, and in community organizing non-electorally, that gets built when we select a location strategically for 20,000-30,000 people. So it involves a lot of different pieces, building power, building accountability — and also building pressure inside Congress, including inside the Democratic Party. One thing that I do think our party needs is a vision that's not just anti-Trump. This is a position I've long held: We actually need a positive, affirmative vision for this country, and be willing to take risks, and vote on real things, and actually tell people what we're fighting for. A lot of people don't know what an average Democrat fights for, because we need to build that consensus. But I don't think that we're starting with nothing. This is where we have an opportunity to fight for minimum wage increases, to fight for guaranteed health care, to fight for something that's actually worthwhile. Like in New York City: in a near historic heat wave, people stood in line in the hot sun, no air conditioning to vote for Zohran. You need to have a vision that's worth standing in the hot sun for hours to vote for. People are not going to do that with only ‘being upset.’ It's important for us to hold the corruption of this administration accountable, but we also have to have an alternative vision. And it can't just be, “Not that.” If it's not that , then what is it? For me, my “what” is raising the federal minimum wage to a living wage. My “what” is guaranteed healthcare for every American and catching up with the rest of the modern world. My “what” is making public colleges and universities tuition free. My “what” is actually having a real path to citizenship in the United States. That’s why this movement is so important: we’re fighting for each and every one of these issues, from universal healthcare to getting money out of our politics. In solidarity, Alexandria | ---|--- | | | ---|---|---|--- Email us: mailto: speak@ocasiocortez.com

Hey folks,
We wrapped another great episode of Endless Urgency, and I had the pleasure of diving into all the chaos with my longtime friend and Senior Fellow at New America, **Ilyse Hogue**. From Trump's bizarre D.C. stunt to the deep fractures inside MAGA, this was one of the most urgent and necessary conversations we've had all year.
🔸 **[07:19]** Ilyse breaks down how Trump's street patrols are exploiting right-wing fear narratives--and why D.C. residents aren't taking the bait.

🔸 **[17:00]** We get into the real reason Trump's pulling these stunts: a smokescreen for scandals like Epstein and economic free fall.

🔸 **[33:10]** The MAGA coalition is cracking, and Ilyse explains why the Epstein files are a political powder keg that could blow it all up.
**[This episode is essential viewing for anyone who refuses to let the far right rewrite the rules unchallenged. Let's stay smart, stay loud, and stay in the fight. With urgency,
--Mike

name: Tim Walz
subject: I’m committed to fighting back alongside the millions of people who don’t see themselves represented in Trump’s America
body:
---
Derek, Every day, we see the disastrous consequences of Trump’s presidency. In the short time he’s been in office, he’s already started to rip away fundamental resources that people rely on — child care, health care, and food assistance — all just to make some of the richest people on Earth even richer. And now, instead of working to lower the cost of living, expanding access to affordable health care, or doing anything to help working families, Trump is directing his MAGA allies in red states to redraw their congressional districts. He’s trying to avoid being held accountable by the voters by putting his thumb on the scales. These are the very real circumstances we are living under right now. **But Derek, we are not powerless.** Millions of us voted against Trump. There are millions of us who won’t stand idly by and let Trump and his allies do whatever they want. We can — _and will_ — fight back against Trump and his allies’ harmful agenda. But to do that, we’ll need a strong, organized Democratic Party leading the way. That’s why I’m reaching out to you today, Derek. The DNC is the organization best positioned to move resources to the places they’re needed most in 2025, 2026, and beyond, as we work to elect the Democrats who will be a check on Trump’s power. **[If holding Trump accountable and electing Democrats at every level matters to you, I’m asking you to chip in $3 today to help:](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250821_4375_fr&amount=3&refcode2=4375_22930333_zQz8ce&akid=4375%2E22930333%2EzQz8ce)**

name: Team WFP
subject: Loud, defiant, and engaged
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Working Families Party PAC (77 Sands St
body:
America isn’t a monarchy, and Trump isn’t a king. | | | Peter, Donald Trump is attempting a hostile takeover of the nation’s capital. Using his emergency powers, he has wrested control from D.C. officials, deployed the National Guard as his personal army, and told police they can “do whatever the hell they want.”1 There are over 700,000 people who call D.C. home — including thousands of Working Families Party members and volunteers. These people deserve to live in peace and safety. **But Donald Trump doesn’t care about keeping our families safe.** If he did, he wouldn’t have defunded D.C. violence prevention programs,2 or pardoned the 1,500 rioters who attacked the Capitol on January 6th.3 If he cared about the wellbeing of D.C. families, he would end the attacks on federal workers, undo his cuts to Medicaid and SNAP, and stop the daily ICE raids that are ripping families apart. **Instead of helping working families, he’s terrorizing them: armored Humvees are patrolling peaceful streets, delivery drivers are being arrested at random, and residents are being interrogated on their own doorsteps. This is what authoritarianism looks like.** These takeovers will not be limited to Los Angeles and D.C. He’s already targeting other major cities by name including New York City, Chicago, Baltimore, and Oakland.4 Now more than ever, we need to be loud, defiant, and engaged. Whether you live in a major city or a small town, talk with your neighbors. Get involved with local organizations. Attend protests and rallies. Write to your elected officials — _urge them_ not to capitulate to Trump’s bullying and threats. **He is counting on us to be too afraid to challenge him. But America isn’t a monarchy, and Trump isn’t a king.** More soon, Team WFP The Working Families Party is fighting tooth and nail to block the far-right and build working-class power around the country. [**Can you pitch in with a contribution? clean_body: America isn’t a monarchy, and Trump isn’t a king. | | | Peter, Donald Trump is attempting a hostile takeover of the nation’s capital. Using his emergency powers, he has wrested control from D.C. officials, deployed the National Guard as his personal army, and told police they can “do whatever the hell they want.”1 There are over 700,000 people who call D.C. home — including thousands of Working Families Party members and volunteers. These people deserve to live in peace and safety. But Donald Trump doesn’t care about keeping our families safe. If he did, he wouldn’t have defunded D.C. violence prevention programs,2 or pardoned the 1,500 rioters who attacked the Capitol on January 6th.3 If he cared about the wellbeing of D.C. families, he would end the attacks on federal workers, undo his cuts to Medicaid and SNAP, and stop the daily ICE raids that are ripping families apart. Instead of helping working families, he’s terrorizing them: armored Humvees are patrolling peaceful streets, delivery drivers are being arrested at random, and residents are being interrogated on their own doorsteps. This is what authoritarianism looks like. These takeovers will not be limited to Los Angeles and D.C. He’s already targeting other major cities by name including New York City, Chicago, Baltimore, and Oakland.4 Now more than ever, we need to be loud, defiant, and engaged. Whether you live in a major city or a small town, talk with your neighbors. Get involved with local organizations. Attend protests and rallies. Write to your elected officials — urge them not to capitulate to Trump’s bullying and threats. He is counting on us to be too afraid to challenge him. But America isn’t a monarchy, and Trump isn’t a king. More soon, Team WFP The Working Families Party is fighting tooth and nail to block the far-right and build working-class power around the country. Sources: 1\. , Rolling Stone, August 11, 2025 2\. , WUSA, May 7, 2025 3\. , January 30, 2025 4\. , August 11, 2025 Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. | [](https://substack.com/redirect/fdbb7ef5-93fe-48ec-9d9b-d5f110745e6b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
_Gov. Tim Walz (D-MN) and Boston Mayor Michelle Wu during an Aug. 15, 2024, campaign appearance in Boston. (Uncredited / Facebook[photo](https://substack.com/redirect/55ec3bf2-fc61-4d8f-8634-f14cbbbc2f0d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).)_
* * *
_**Cliff 's Note: **This guest column originally[ appeared](https://substack.com/redirect/ba4d98db-db3b-44b5-83e8-35963b59a857?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at "The Fucking News," the Substack of journalist Jonathan Larsen, Be prepared, there'll be "Fucks" in this piece, as it's written in a satirical style I fully endorse, and think is sorely needed right now. Larsen's a veteran of ABC, CNN and MSNBC, where he served as exec producer of Up with Chris Hayes. _
* * *
Thirty-five states and cities just told Attorney General **Pam Bondi** to fuck off and to do it all the way. Last week, Bondi wrote to them ordering them to start helping Pres. **Donald Trump** 's hunt for immigrants including by dropping so-called sanctuary laws prohibiting such help.
Responses to Bondi obtained by Politico show Democratic leaders telling Bondi she can stick her demands up her sanctuary for poop.
Bondi claimed falsely that U.S. law requires states and municipalities to do whatever executive branch officials tell them to do, including task police and other law enforcement to help out with Trump's bullshit immigrant hunt.
Bondi threatened retribution -- including prosecution and less funding for everything except federal forces in the streets -- against states that don't comply.
The 35 responses Politico obtained ranged in tone from "fuck off" to "kindly fuck off pursuant to relevant statute."
Bondi had given a deadline of Tuesday for state and local leaders to respond and Politico reported on their substance on Wednesday.
Here's how Gov. **Tim Walz** (D-MN), the former vice presidential candidate, politely closes his letter informing Bondi that off is how she can fuck:
> > That the federal government would insist that Minnesota should divert state resources to do the federal government's job or help effectuate some kind of misguided political agenda is fundamentally inconsistent with our founding principles as a nation.
>> I firmly believe that securing our border does not mean we sacrifice our constitutional values; I urge you to join me in rejecting that false choice.
Befitting a college town, Boston Mayor **Michelle Wu** got all academic on Bondi's ass in her own [letter](https://substack.com/redirect/d4282df9-f305-4a9f-9ce3-1995e3632162?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Wu tried teaching the nation's top law-enforcement officer about laws. And history:
> > The City of Boston follows the law -- local, state, and federal. The United States Constitution establishes that local governments, closest to the people, hold the authority to enact public safety policies without the threat of unlawful interference from the federal government.
>> The Founding Fathers included these protections as a direct result of the attempted coercion of Boston by an unaccountable and distant monarch, and this city's refusal to bow down to tyranny.
And in case remote learning of American history wasn't enough, Wu took Bondi to school:
> > This federal administration's false and continuous attacks on American cities and millions of our residents are unprecedented. You have eliminated health care and food assistance for our families; unlawfully cancelled grants for our schools and roads; slashed funding for our universities, hospitals, and research institutions; and deployed military personnel to occupy our streets.
>> These attacks all come back to a common aim: the Trump Administration seeks to divide, isolate, and intimidate our cities, and make Americans fearful of one another.
The Democratic leaders who advised Bondi on her off-fucking options include Govs. **Bob Ferguson** (D-WA), **Kathy Hochul** (D-NY), **Tina Kotek** (D-OR), **Gavin Newsom** (D-CA), and **JB Pritzker** (D-IL), and Mayors **Eric Adams** (New York City), **Ravi Bhalla** (Hoboken), **Malik D. Evans** (Rochester, NY), **Bruce Harrell** (Seattle) and **Mike Johnston** (Denver).
Since cities and states don't get to make immigration policy, forcing them to fund it with taxpayer dollars would be weirdly similar to taxation without representation. Which, history tells us, is capable of inspiring revolution.
Of course, it's not just the legal and constitutional principles at work here. There are _reasons_ cities and states don't maximize every opportunity to go after undocumented immigrants. For one thing, there might simply be better things for cops to do: Like pursue actual criminals in the hope that Trump won't pardon them or cover up their crimes like he did with **Jeffrey Epstein**.
Also, turning cops into immigration stormtroopers _hurts_ communities. Not just by tearing apart the law-abiding fabric of those communities, aka humans, but also by sowing fear of law enforcement, discouraging cooperation on crimefighting that _actually_ affects peace and safety.
In other words, officials cooperating __ with Trump are shredding their communities and helping actual criminals (mostly citizens!) evade detection.
Bondi herself appears to be committing legal no-nos, albeit not as dire as her felonious boss's record.
Interestingly, it was two _cities_ that put Bondi on notice about a possible ethics violation on top of her mountain of ethics violations. Hoboken, NJ, and Rochester, NY, told Bondi that because she's already suing their mayors it was unethical for her to communicate directly with those mayors and not with their counsel, without counsel's permission.
Bhalla of Hoboken literally responded, "I am requesting that you immediately cease and desist all direct communication with me" on this issue.
Hilariously, the overworked and newly dumb Justice Department said that because "every letter was addressed to the legal representative of the official responsible for these dangerous sanctuary policies, there is no basis for any complaint."
The letters were actually cc'ed to counsel, but the Justice Department misses the entire point: They didn't seek counsel's _permission_ to communicate directly with the local leaders they're suing. So this may end up before some unlucky bar association somewhere...
...Just like Bondi's attempt to enforce Trump's unconstitutional commandeering of state and local resources is gonna land her in court. Where she doesn't have [enough good lawyers](https://substack.com/redirect/76b8e380-ad0c-4dd5-8b3f-67331fe197a6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to defend her.
And it's not just unethics, it's ineptitude. Reportedly, Bondi has named sanctuaries that don't even have sanctuary laws. In some cases, frantically revising her lists after her fuck-ups were fact-fucked.
And remember, members of Congress, no matter what party, are supposed to represent their constituents in these areas. Upholding the Constitution as they vowed to do includes guarding the checks-and-balances autonomy of states and cities.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.Ok39dCGTCFyjS47vM8sinzOdKwQVegS5dr5yraGUJ5o?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=171610703)
#### **BUT WAIT, THERE 'S MORE!...**
#### **Putin Adds Vicious Noogie-ing to Recent Trump Swirly**
Not done humiliating Pres. **Donald Trump** by publicly urinating on everything Trump proudly announced after his Ukraine summits on Friday and Monday, Russia yesterday took away all of Trump's participation trophies and refused to give them back.
Specifically, Russian Pres. **Vladimir Putin** is not only saying no to a face-to-face sit-down with Ukrainian Pres. **Volodymyr Zelenskyy** , he's now pooping on those security guarantees that Trump thought Putin was gonna let him give Ukraine.
"It's been explicitly stated: NO NATO troops as peacekeepers. Russia won't accept such a 'security guarantee'," former Russian president **Dmitry Medvedev** wrote. Also, Russian Foreign Minister **Sergei Lavrov** said Russia wants veto power over any measures Trump and Europe come up with for Ukraine's future defense against Russia's future offense.
Historians may recall that the Trump administration defended his Friday summit with Putin -- despite not getting a ceasefire first -- because it was important to find out what Putin wants. And yet, despite supposedly finding that out at the summit, Russia's still fucking around, meaning Trump has now achieved the first recorded reversal of the traditional process of fucking around and _then_ finding out.
Trump is now expected to threaten sanctions and maybe give Putin two weeks to clarify what he wants.
**FLASHBACK** So, um, what the zorching fuck did Trump and Putin even talk about on Friday? **Jeffrey Epstein**?

clean_body: Attorney general warned 35 Democratic officials to get rid of immigration "sanctuaries" and they just responded that she can get bent | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? Attorney general warned 35 Democratic officials to get rid of immigration "sanctuaries" and they just responded that she can get bent | | | Aug 22 | | | | | | | | | | | | 🔵 Don 't Let Corporate & Right Wing Media Rig Our Political System! Get A Paid Subscription to Support our Soundcheck Fund so we can continue to give grants to Independent-media creators! | | ---|---|--- Gov. Tim Walz (D-MN) and Boston Mayor Michelle Wu during an Aug. 15, 2024, campaign appearance in Boston. (Uncredited / Facebook.) Cliff 's Note: This guest column originally at "The Fucking News," the Substack of journalist Jonathan Larsen, Be prepared, there'll be "Fucks" in this piece, as it's written in a satirical style I fully endorse, and think is sorely needed right now. Larsen's a veteran of ABC, CNN and MSNBC, where he served as exec producer of Up with Chris Hayes. Thirty-five states and cities just told Attorney General Pam Bondi to fuck off and to do it all the way. Last week, Bondi wrote to them ordering them to start helping Pres. Donald Trump 's hunt for immigrants including by dropping so-called sanctuary laws prohibiting such help. Responses to Bondi obtained by Politico show Democratic leaders telling Bondi she can stick her demands up her sanctuary for poop. Bondi claimed falsely that U.S. law requires states and municipalities to do whatever executive branch officials tell them to do, including task police and other law enforcement to help out with Trump's bullshit immigrant hunt. Bondi threatened retribution -- including prosecution and less funding for everything except federal forces in the streets -- against states that don't comply. The 35 responses Politico obtained ranged in tone from "fuck off" to "kindly fuck off pursuant to relevant statute." Bondi had given a deadline of Tuesday for state and local leaders to respond and Politico reported on their substance on Wednesday. Here's how Gov. Tim Walz (D-MN), the former vice presidential candidate, politely closes his letter informing Bondi that off is how she can fuck: > That the federal government would insist that Minnesota should divert state resources to do the federal government's job or help effectuate some kind of misguided political agenda is fundamentally inconsistent with our founding principles as a nation. > I firmly believe that securing our border does not mean we sacrifice our constitutional values; I urge you to join me in rejecting that false choice. Befitting a college town, Boston Mayor Michelle Wu got all academic on Bondi's ass in her own . Wu tried teaching the nation's top law-enforcement officer about laws. And history: > The City of Boston follows the law -- local, state, and federal. The United States Constitution establishes that local governments, closest to the people, hold the authority to enact public safety policies without the threat of unlawful interference from the federal government. > The Founding Fathers included these protections as a direct result of the attempted coercion of Boston by an unaccountable and distant monarch, and this city's refusal to bow down to tyranny. And in case remote learning of American history wasn't enough, Wu took Bondi to school: > This federal administration's false and continuous attacks on American cities and millions of our residents are unprecedented. You have eliminated health care and food assistance for our families; unlawfully cancelled grants for our schools and roads; slashed funding for our universities, hospitals, and research institutions; and deployed military personnel to occupy our streets. > These attacks all come back to a common aim: the Trump Administration seeks to divide, isolate, and intimidate our cities, and make Americans fearful of one another. The Democratic leaders who advised Bondi on her off-fucking options include Govs. Bob Ferguson (D-WA), Kathy Hochul (D-NY), Tina Kotek (D-OR), Gavin Newsom (D-CA), and JB Pritzker (D-IL), and Mayors Eric Adams (New York City), Ravi Bhalla (Hoboken), Malik D. Evans (Rochester, NY), Bruce Harrell (Seattle) and Mike Johnston (Denver). Since cities and states don't get to make immigration policy, forcing them to fund it with taxpayer dollars would be weirdly similar to taxation without representation. Which, history tells us, is capable of inspiring revolution. Of course, it's not just the legal and constitutional principles at work here. There are reasons cities and states don't maximize every opportunity to go after undocumented immigrants. For one thing, there might simply be better things for cops to do: Like pursue actual criminals in the hope that Trump won't pardon them or cover up their crimes like he did with Jeffrey Epstein. Also, turning cops into immigration stormtroopers hurts communities. Not just by tearing apart the law-abiding fabric of those communities, aka humans, but also by sowing fear of law enforcement, discouraging cooperation on crimefighting that actually affects peace and safety. In other words, officials cooperating with Trump are shredding their communities and helping actual criminals (mostly citizens!) evade detection. Bondi herself appears to be committing legal no-nos, albeit not as dire as her felonious boss's record. Interestingly, it was two cities that put Bondi on notice about a possible ethics violation on top of her mountain of ethics violations. Hoboken, NJ, and Rochester, NY, told Bondi that because she's already suing their mayors it was unethical for her to communicate directly with those mayors and not with their counsel, without counsel's permission. Bhalla of Hoboken literally responded, "I am requesting that you immediately cease and desist all direct communication with me" on this issue. Hilariously, the overworked and newly dumb Justice Department said that because "every letter was addressed to the legal representative of the official responsible for these dangerous sanctuary policies, there is no basis for any complaint." The letters were actually cc'ed to counsel, but the Justice Department misses the entire point: They didn't seek counsel's permission to communicate directly with the local leaders they're suing. So this may end up before some unlucky bar association somewhere... ...Just like Bondi's attempt to enforce Trump's unconstitutional commandeering of state and local resources is gonna land her in court. Where she doesn't have to defend her. And it's not just unethics, it's ineptitude. Reportedly, Bondi has named sanctuaries that don't even have sanctuary laws. In some cases, frantically revising her lists after her fuck-ups were fact-fucked. And remember, members of Congress, no matter what party, are supposed to represent their constituents in these areas. Upholding the Constitution as they vowed to do includes guarding the checks-and-balances autonomy of states and cities. BUT WAIT, THERE 'S MORE!... Putin Adds Vicious Noogie-ing to Recent Trump Swirly Not done humiliating Pres. Donald Trump by publicly urinating on everything Trump proudly announced after his Ukraine summits on Friday and Monday, Russia yesterday took away all of Trump's participation trophies and refused to give them back. Specifically, Russian Pres. Vladimir Putin is not only saying no to a face-to-face sit-down with Ukrainian Pres. Volodymyr Zelenskyy , he's now pooping on those security guarantees that Trump thought Putin was gonna let him give Ukraine. "It's been explicitly stated: NO NATO troops as peacekeepers. Russia won't accept such a 'security guarantee'," former Russian president Dmitry Medvedev wrote. Also, Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov said Russia wants veto power over any measures Trump and Europe come up with for Ukraine's future defense against Russia's future offense. Historians may recall that the Trump administration defended his Friday summit with Putin -- despite not getting a ceasefire first -- because it was important to find out what Putin wants. And yet, despite supposedly finding that out at the summit, Russia's still fucking around, meaning Trump has now achieved the first recorded reversal of the traditional process of fucking around and then finding out. Trump is now expected to threaten sanctions and maybe give Putin two weeks to clarify what he wants. FLASHBACK So, um, what the zorching fuck did Trump and Putin even talk about on Friday? Jeffrey Epstein? 🎥 VIDEO ON DEMAND! 🔵 Support Independent Media. Save OurDemocracy!** You're currently a free subscriber to . name: Team Gillibrand
subject: A rock and a hard place
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
Despite everything that’s going on with Donald Trump, it’s important that we keep pushing to make life more affordable for Americans.
Right now, millions of working parents are stuck between a rock and a hard place: Child care costs are skyrocketing – if they can even find a spot. Providers are closing their doors. Families are being forced to make impossible choices about their jobs, their bills, and their futures. **It doesn’t have to be this way.** Senator Kirsten Gillibrand _just_ introduced bipartisan legislation to expand affordable child care, help parents get back to work and give providers the support they need to keep their doors open. [**_Now we need to show just how many Americans agree. Add your name to support urgent action to address the child care crisis._**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113761847/564866303/1473692499?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.08.22_Affordable-Childcare_B2_F1_S1_C2_45AC_N-T2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjZkMDZlYy1mMTdlLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kytfArDrlvbxxUsU9dUSNWRTTcOO25PEr9wc8yQ6uVc=&emci=eb60580f-c67e-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=c66d06ec-f17e-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=7659029) | | [ADD YOUR NAME >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113761849/564866304/1473692499?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.08.22_Affordable-Childcare_B2_F1_S1_C2_45AC_N-T2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjZkMDZlYy1mMTdlLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kytfArDrlvbxxUsU9dUSNWRTTcOO25PEr9wc8yQ6uVc=&emci=eb60580f-c67e-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=c66d06ec-f17e-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=7659029)

We know that affordable, accessible child care is the foundation for a strong economy and thriving families. But unfortunately, it’s become out of reach and unaffordable for too many families.
* The average cost of child care is $14,000 per year.
* Almost half of the country lives in a child care “desert” — with no direct access to care.
* And the US loses billions in lost economic activity due to this lack of support.

Every signature is critical. By signing, you are sending a message that Americans demand action to make child care affordable for every family.
[**_Actions like this will go a long way. Will you sign your name?_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113761851/564866307/1473692499?refcode=LOC_EM_PET_2025.08.22_Affordable-Childcare_B2_F1_S1_C2_45AC_N-T2&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJjNjZkMDZlYy1mMTdlLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=kytfArDrlvbxxUsU9dUSNWRTTcOO25PEr9wc8yQ6uVc=&emci=eb60580f-c67e-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=c66d06ec-f17e-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=7659029)

clean_body: Despite everything that’s going on with Donald Trump, it’s important that we keep pushing to make life more affordable for Americans. Right now, millions of working parents are stuck between a rock and a hard place: Child care costs are skyrocketing – if they can even find a spot. Providers are closing their doors. Families are being forced to make impossible choices about their jobs, their bills, and their futures. It doesn’t have to be this way. Senator Kirsten Gillibrand just introduced bipartisan legislation to expand affordable child care, help parents get back to work and give providers the support they need to keep their doors open. | | We know that affordable, accessible child care is the foundation for a strong economy and thriving families. But unfortunately, it’s become out of reach and unaffordable for too many families. The average cost of child care is $14,000 per year. Almost half of the country lives in a child care “desert” — with no direct access to care. And the US loses billions in lost economic activity due to this lack of support. Every signature is critical. By signing, you are sending a message that Americans demand action to make child care affordable for every family. | — Team Gillibrand =

Friend,
Tonight, outside a School Board meeting in Northern Virginia with Lt. Governor Winsome Earle-Sears, a Virginia Democrat proudly held up a sign that read:
_"HEY WINSOME, IF TRANS CAN’T SHARE YOUR BATHROOM THEN BLACKS CAN’T SHARE MY WATER FOUNTAIN"_
Let’s call this what it is: **racist, hateful, and disgusting.**
This is the so-called “tolerance” of the Left on full display. They claim to fight for inclusion, yet the moment they disagree with a conservative Black woman, their mask slips—and their true colors come out.
**This is exactly what we are up against:** a radical movement that will stoop to racism and hate to silence anyone who dares to stand for common sense, faith, and freedom.
**But Winsome will not be intimidated.**_**Neither will we.**_
Now is the time to fight back—louder, stronger, and with more resolve than ever before. Will you stand with us today?

clean_body: | | | | What Virginia Democrats are saying... Friend, Tonight, outside a School Board meeting in Northern Virginia with Lt. Governor Winsome Earle-Sears, a Virginia Democrat proudly held up a sign that read: "HEY WINSOME, IF TRANS CAN’T SHARE YOUR BATHROOM THEN BLACKS CAN’T SHARE MY WATER FOUNTAIN" Let’s call this what it is: racist, hateful, and disgusting. This is the so-called “tolerance” of the Left on full display. They claim to fight for inclusion, yet the moment they disagree with a conservative Black woman, their mask slips—and their true colors come out. This is exactly what we are up against: a radical movement that will stoop to racism and hate to silence anyone who dares to stand for common sense, faith, and freedom. But Winsome will not be intimidated.Neither will we. Now is the time to fight back—louder, stronger, and with more resolve than ever before. Will you stand with us today? | | | Every dollar helps us push back against the Left’s hypocrisy and defend leaders like Winsome Earle-Sears who represent the values of hardworking Virginians. Thank you for standing strong, RPV Rapid Response | == \- PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY THE REPUBLICAN PARTY OF VIRGINIA

Peter,

The Democrats hate me. The media lies about me. And now even the GOP insiders are trying to silence me. They want me to follow their protocol, bow to their donors, and shut my mouth.

I won’t.

If we’re going to fix Minnesota — if we’re going to stop the crime, the corruption, and the rot — we need to do it with you. Not the party. Not the elites. Not the lobbyists. You.

That’s why I’m running — not to play the game, but to END it. They’re counting on me being alone. **[Prove them wrong.](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=71b59306acbb4b63aff82fc3c0677cfa&_e=LdF2XKrgXvPKqoZytmQhf4grOUHhiD_Tj7ky3B761DaoWTXe9gORrgvDrnZ_qbvXa6PyTGuC4lpfxr9MOJngKM6P9Kxw0ySfirWCbNFMlTFloYAVZG0jW155xDDW7HiXK8Zy5J4N0YAYgBo3cYvqTaHe-7DwtfdpSrCkZ5gkobCI8JGolYnx9b6WO2C0_6IEu0EdKzg5OcHEotSbMNXNxNZ83mobsZkQYKkKgSDEM_HmHnLE8PmB1Hz5AnUD4ljEOL8L_b3Ci_GgVXF5WFNKsD7C2UZrj2c1s80RKLrr-fKRIckUC8Va5afX4oRnmNop2Q5Qkuu53dnPbvez5_ioN7auVxd7OFRSrnKQw-p57Vu2dF5g7d-bl2JFM3WmpA0qXSsd_GBIsOC3gIrlsqrxteucTXnGJhUznqAAbo8sceUPUJr0VKN_tsoEDV8Omu6H1AFQX2e7H6FcHUi5BZAUYRN2A_RiINPQ6pq_v8KynRzRe8z007cX3VO9ircBy8dFGaIBahaW7_GVGA78VOHB_iUfjIyCftwZcvxqjXEPHcOrnccCDn7Fm7yQPXrTo13F)**

name: All On The Line
subject: The Voting Rights Act is in jeopardy
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND
body:
We need your support to push back against these attacks
|
---

From extreme gerrymandering in Texas to attacks on the Voting Rights Act at the nation’s highest court, the fair maps movement is fighting existential threats to democracy on multiple fronts.
**See our breakdown below of everything that’s at stake when the Supreme Court hears arguments on whether or not the cornerstone law of voting rights in America is constitutional, and[make a donation to fund our urgent grassroots work to fight back against threats to democracy.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/113762039/564872988/174172392?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_TopperVRA_email_20250821-AOTL-Tier1-TopperVRA_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250821-AOTL-Tier1-TopperVRA_&utm_campaign=TopperVRA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk2OWEyMjU1LWYzN2UtZjAxMS1iNDg0LTYwNDViZGViNzQxMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=OPeIiIqQwW6JCBmHjtrsAloe47dMELNUM-_r5ZajrdE=)**
\---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: All On The Line
Date: Fri, Aug 8, 2025
Subject: The Voting Rights Act is at risk
Team,
This week was the 60th anniversary of the Voting Rights Act — the crown jewel of the Civil Rights Movement. The Voting Rights Act makes America a better nation by expanding and protecting voters’ access to the ballot, particularly voters of color.
**But right now, its future is in danger. Sixty years after its passage and after decades of courts affirming it, the Supreme Court just announced it would consider whether the Voting Rights Act is constitutional.**
In a moment when our democracy is under unprecedented, extreme electoral attacks, the most effective law at protecting the right to vote in American history is at risk. _This is an all-hands-on-deck moment for our movement._
**[Make a donation today to help All On The Line keep up the fight to protect voting rights at every level – from the Texas legislature to far beyond.](https://click.everyaction.com/k/113762040/564872989/174172392?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_TopperVRA_email_20250821-AOTL-Tier1-TopperVRA_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250821-AOTL-Tier1-TopperVRA_&utm_campaign=TopperVRA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk2OWEyMjU1LWYzN2UtZjAxMS1iNDg0LTYwNDViZGViNzQxMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=OPeIiIqQwW6JCBmHjtrsAloe47dMELNUM-_r5ZajrdE=)**
You might remember that the Supreme Court decided to rehear _Louisiana v. Callais,_ the case involving Louisiana’s congressional map. Originally, the map included just one Black-opportunity district out of the state’s six, despite the fact that African Americans make up one-third of the state of Louisiana. Two years ago, the same Supreme Court ruled in _Allen v. Milligan_ that a similar map in Alabama was in violation of the Voting Rights Act — prompting a lower court to order Louisiana to redraw its map to include two Black-opportunity districts.
Now the Supreme Court wants to take another look, announcing it wants to consider whether the redrawn, fairer map violates the 14th Amendment rights of non-Black citizens. Put another way, we will soon see arguments in front of the highest court in the land that the _Voting Rights Act itself is unconstitutional._
**For anti-democracy extremists, this is their end goal: To gut the Voting Rights Act and run rampant with gerrymandering. They’re actively fighting this on all fronts — from redrawing Texas’s already-gerrymandered maps to gutting the Voting Rights Act through the courts.**
[**That’s why we have to fight back against this extremism with all our strength. In this existential moment for our democracy — and on the 60th anniversary of the Voting Rights Act — make a donation to fund our grassroots movement and fight back with everything we’ve got.**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/113762041/564872990/174172392?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_TopperVRA_email_20250821-AOTL-Tier1-TopperVRA_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20250821-AOTL-Tier1-TopperVRA_&utm_campaign=TopperVRA&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjk2OWEyMjU1LWYzN2UtZjAxMS1iNDg0LTYwNDViZGViNzQxMyIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=OPeIiIqQwW6JCBmHjtrsAloe47dMELNUM-_r5ZajrdE=)

clean_body: We need your support to push back against these attacks | From extreme gerrymandering in Texas to attacks on the Voting Rights Act at the nation’s highest court, the fair maps movement is fighting existential threats to democracy on multiple fronts. See our breakdown below of everything that’s at stake when the Supreme Court hears arguments on whether or not the cornerstone law of voting rights in America is constitutional, and= \---------- Forwarded message --------- From: All On The Line Date: Fri, Aug 8, 2025 Subject: The Voting Rights Act is at risk Team, This week was the 60th anniversary of the Voting Rights Act — the crown jewel of the Civil Rights Movement. The Voting Rights Act makes America a better nation by expanding and protecting voters’ access to the ballot, particularly voters of color. But right now, its future is in danger. Sixty years after its passage and after decades of courts affirming it, the Supreme Court just announced it would consider whether the Voting Rights Act is constitutional. In a moment when our democracy is under unprecedented, extreme electoral attacks, the most effective law at protecting the right to vote in American history is at risk. This is an all-hands-on-deck moment for our movement. = You might remember that the Supreme Court decided to rehear Louisiana v. Callais, the case involving Louisiana’s congressional map. Originally, the map included just one Black-opportunity district out of the state’s six, despite the fact that African Americans make up one-third of the state of Louisiana. Two years ago, the same Supreme Court ruled in Allen v. Milligan that a similar map in Alabama was in violation of the Voting Rights Act — prompting a lower court to order Louisiana to redraw its map to include two Black-opportunity districts. Now the Supreme Court wants to take another look, announcing it wants to consider whether the redrawn, fairer map violates the 14th Amendment rights of non-Black citizens. Put another way, we will soon see arguments in front of the highest court in the land that the Voting Rights Act itself is unconstitutional. For anti-democracy extremists, this is their end goal: To gut the Voting Rights Act and run rampant with gerrymandering. They’re actively fighting this on all fronts — from redrawing Texas’s already-gerrymandered maps to gutting the Voting Rights Act through the courts. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thank you for being a part of this all-out defense of our democracy. — All On The Line is the grassroots advocacy campaign supported by the National Redistricting Action Fund. Support our work to end gerrymandering. Text FAIR to 88228 to receive recurring updates from AOTL by automated text message. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. OPINION: Parents Outraged as Lyons Township Civics Assignment Pushes Political Labels on 16-Year-Olds (https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/08/opinion-parents-outraged-as-lyons-township-civics-assignment-pushes-political-labels-on-16-year-olds.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=5460588ba6-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823)
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/08/opinion-parents-outraged-as-lyons-township-civics-assignment-pushes-political-labels-on-16-year-olds.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=5460588ba6-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
Lyons Township High School South Campus. (Scott Anderson/Patch)
By Janelle Powell, Opinion Contributor

Parents send their children to school to learn math, science, history, and civics – not to be pawns in a political mind game. Yet at Lyons Township High School, sophomores were handed a so-called “non-political quiz” in civics class that looks more like an exercise in political conditioning than education.

The assignment, titled “Liberal vs. Conservative Style Non-Political Quiz,” presents students with a series of questions about trivial school-related scenarios – cafeteria food, classroom seating, sports uniforms, school mascots – and forces them into binary “liberal” or “conservative” categories.

For example:

“Should the cafeteria try new foods? Answer A: Liberal. Answer B: Conservative”

“Should sports teams change uniforms sometimes? Answer A: Liberal. Answer B: Conservative”

“Should the school mascot be changed? Answer A: Liberal. Answer B: Conservative”
https://www.illinoisreview.com/illinoisreview/2025/08/opinion-parents-outraged-as-lyons-township-civics-assignment-pushes-political-labels-on-16-year-olds.html?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=5460588ba6-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823
A copy of the quiz provided by a Lyons Township parent.
This is presented to 15 and 16-year-old students as a civics lesson. The problem? These questions have absolutely nothing to do with actual liberalism or conservatism in the American civic or political sense. They are arbitrary and misleading parallels that equate curiosity, creativity, or even preference for variety with being a “liberal,” while equating tradition or consistency with being “conservative.”

In other words: if your child prefers the same lunch menu every week, they’re “conservative.” If they like trying a new sandwich, suddenly they’re “liberal.”

This is not education – it’s indoctrination.

The Parent Perspective

One parent I spoke too, who wants to remain nameless, was furious:

“What the hell does liberal and conservative even have to do with the mascot and lunch food at Lyons Township? That teacher is trying to brainwash them to think that their totally logical thoughts about high school make them liberal.”

Exactly.

This is the danger – conditioning young students to see normal preferences and personality traits through a false political lens. And worse, doing so under the guise of a neutral civics lesson, without parents’ knowledge.

Civics, Not Social Engineering

The goal of civics education should be to teach the Constitution, the Bill of Rights, how government functions, and why civic responsibility matters. It should not be to manipulate teenagers into political identity boxes through cafeteria hypotheticals and mascot debates.

Parents need to know this is happening in their children’s classrooms. Thankfully, there are options. Illinois families do not have to accept indoctrination disguised as curriculum.

What Parents Can Do

Now is not the time to stop paying attention to what your kids are learning in high school. These years are some of the most formative – and potentially the most detrimental – of their lives. You need to be a part of it.

Ask questions. Be present. And most importantly: make your kids show you their assignments.

Groups like Awake Illinois provide resources and guidance on how parents can opt their children out of this type of political conditioning and restore civics education to what it’s supposed to be – education, not indoctrination.

Visit
Awake Illinois (https://www.awakeil.com/?utm_source=Illinois+Review&utm_campaign=5460588ba6-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2023_10_30_09_24_COPY_01&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_-8b40ac336f-666344823) to learn more about your options and how to push back. Our kids deserve civics lessons grounded in truth and history – not politically loaded quizzes that sort teenagers into categories they never asked to be put in.

The teacher responsible for distributing this assignment could not be reached for comment.

clean_body: view in browser () | update preferences () | unsubscribe () This IR e-Blast Powered By: Dear Peter, We thought you may be interested to receive this breaking news alert. Illinois Review is the largest conservative media source in Illinois, and the only source in Illinois that has been shared by President Donald Trump. Twitter () Facebook () IllinoisReview.com () OPINION: Parents Outraged as Lyons Township Civics Assignment Pushes Political Labels on 16-Year-Olds () Lyons Township High School South Campus. (Scott Anderson/Patch) By Janelle Powell, Opinion Contributor Parents send their children to school to learn math, science, history, and civics – not to be pawns in a political mind game. Yet at Lyons Township High School, sophomores were handed a so-called “non-political quiz” in civics class that looks more like an exercise in political conditioning than education. The assignment, titled “Liberal vs. Conservative Style Non-Political Quiz,” presents students with a series of questions about trivial school-related scenarios – cafeteria food, classroom seating, sports uniforms, school mascots – and forces them into binary “liberal” or “conservative” categories. For example: “Should the cafeteria try new foods? Answer A: Liberal. Answer B: Conservative” “Should sports teams change uniforms sometimes? Answer A: Liberal. Answer B: Conservative” “Should the school mascot be changed? Answer A: Liberal. Answer B: Conservative” A copy of the quiz provided by a Lyons Township parent. This is presented to 15 and 16-year-old students as a civics lesson. The problem? These questions have absolutely nothing to do with actual liberalism or conservatism in the American civic or political sense. They are arbitrary and misleading parallels that equate curiosity, creativity, or even preference for variety with being a “liberal,” while equating tradition or consistency with being “conservative.” In other words: if your child prefers the same lunch menu every week, they’re “conservative.” If they like trying a new sandwich, suddenly they’re “liberal.” This is not education – it’s indoctrination. The Parent Perspective One parent I spoke too, who wants to remain nameless, was furious: “What the hell does liberal and conservative even have to do with the mascot and lunch food at Lyons Township? That teacher is trying to brainwash them to think that their totally logical thoughts about high school make them liberal.” Exactly. This is the danger – conditioning young students to see normal preferences and personality traits through a false political lens. And worse, doing so under the guise of a neutral civics lesson, without parents’ knowledge. Civics, Not Social Engineering The goal of civics education should be to teach the Constitution, the Bill of Rights, how government functions, and why civic responsibility matters. It should not be to manipulate teenagers into political identity boxes through cafeteria hypotheticals and mascot debates. Parents need to know this is happening in their children’s classrooms. Thankfully, there are options. Illinois families do not have to accept indoctrination disguised as curriculum. What Parents Can Do Now is not the time to stop paying attention to what your kids are learning in high school. These years are some of the most formative – and potentially the most detrimental – of their lives. You need to be a part of it. Ask questions. Be present. And most importantly: make your kids show you their assignments. Groups like Awake Illinois provide resources and guidance on how parents can opt their children out of this type of political conditioning and restore civics education to what it’s supposed to be – education, not indoctrination. Visit Awake Illinois () to learn more about your options and how to push back. Our kids deserve civics lessons grounded in truth and history – not politically loaded quizzes that sort teenagers into categories they never asked to be put in. The teacher responsible for distributing this assignment could not be reached for comment. * **TOP NEWS**

**" Hill Country nonprofit pledges $40 million to help rebuild flood victims' homes," **Texas Tribune's**Colleen DeGuzman** \-- "A Hill Country nonprofit announced Thursday that it's pledging $40 million to help rebuild homes in Kerr County, the grief-stricken community hit the hardest by the July 4 weekend flash floods that devastated neighborhoods, summer camps and RV parks.

The Community Foundation of the Texas Hill Country has pledged the amount as the first step to its four-pronged recovery plan: rebuilding homes, supporting mental health, economic recovery, and reviving the area's quality of life.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/5a4c349d-6bef-451b-896a-3bd7e26caaaa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Community Foundation CEO **Austin Dickson** said the organization is starting with housing efforts "because rebuilding a safe, stable place to call home is at the heart of recovery .... This is more than bricks and lumber.

"Whether it's a rebuilt house, a repaired RV or a safe temporary apartment, this fund will help families get home," he later added.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/5a4c349d-6bef-451b-896a-3bd7e26caaaa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Dickson spoke alongside Gov. **Greg Abbott** and Hill Country leaders from a park in Kerrville -- most of the 137 Central Texas flood victims died in Kerr County. The park is about a 30-minute drive from Camp Mystic, where 27 campers and counselors died in the flood that struck in the early morning hours on July 4.

Tightening camp safety and securing relief funding are among the highest priorities of state lawmakers this special legislative session, Abbott said.

In Austin, the Texas House on Thursday passed six bills related to flood and disaster relief, including [a proposal](https://substack.com/redirect/91d8cc9d-26d2-4d04-9a3b-c2b8a3516f76?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to set aside hundreds of millions of state dollars to fund recovery efforts.

While lawmakers are discussing flood relief and response at a statewide level, Dickson said local leaders are doing what they can to jump start the recovery process. The Community Foundation on Thursday also announced that it has created a local advisory council made up of community members who will "guide funding decisions." It didn't release the names of the members Thursday.

Hunt ISD Superintendent **Luci Harmon** said rebuilding homes will help students, whose first day of the school year was Wednesday, get back to some sense of normalcy.

"Our students thrive when their housing situation is under control -- when they're in their homes and their homes are stable," she said.

The Community Foundation has also allocated $1.6 million to covering short-term housing for flood victims through AirBnB's nonprofit, [AirBnB.org](https://substack.com/redirect/3ac8b5d1-7aaf-42db-bae4-4985c9314521?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), so families have a place to stay while their houses get rebuilt." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/7e4e23d8-664f-4303-99c3-2c3f7c83e829?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

TOP NEWS " Hill Country nonprofit pledges $40 million to help rebuild flood victims' homes," Texas Tribune'sColleen DeGuzman \-- "A Hill Country nonprofit announced Thursday that it's pledging $40 million to help rebuild homes in Kerr County, the grief-stricken community hit the hardest by the July 4 weekend flash floods that devastated neighborhoods, summer camps and RV parks. The Community Foundation of the Texas Hill Country has pledged the amount as the first step to its four-pronged recovery plan: rebuilding homes, supporting mental health, economic recovery, and reviving the area's quality of life. Community Foundation CEO Austin Dickson said the organization is starting with housing efforts "because rebuilding a safe, stable place to call home is at the heart of recovery .... This is more than bricks and lumber. "Whether it's a rebuilt house, a repaired RV or a safe temporary apartment, this fund will help families get home," he later added. Dickson spoke alongside Gov. Greg Abbott and Hill Country leaders from a park in Kerrville -- most of the 137 Central Texas flood victims died in Kerr County. The park is about a 30-minute drive from Camp Mystic, where 27 campers and counselors died in the flood that struck in the early morning hours on July 4. Tightening camp safety and securing relief funding are among the highest priorities of state lawmakers this special legislative session, Abbott said. In Austin, the Texas House on Thursday passed six bills related to flood and disaster relief, including to set aside hundreds of millions of state dollars to fund recovery efforts. While lawmakers are discussing flood relief and response at a statewide level, Dickson said local leaders are doing what they can to jump start the recovery process. The Community Foundation on Thursday also announced that it has created a local advisory council made up of community members who will "guide funding decisions." It didn't release the names of the members Thursday. Hunt ISD Superintendent Luci Harmon said rebuilding homes will help students, whose first day of the school year was Wednesday, get back to some sense of normalcy. "Our students thrive when their housing situation is under control -- when they're in their homes and their homes are stable," she said. The Community Foundation has also allocated $1.6 million to covering short-term housing for flood victims through AirBnB's nonprofit, , so families have a place to stay while their houses get rebuilt." FREE SUBSCRIPTIONS HAVE ENDED : As of July 11, 2022, we have moved to a paywall for free subscribers. Free subscribers will only receive the top story in their morning email. Free subscribers will need to become paid subscribers to receive full story summaries. * **TOP NEWS**

**" Hill Country nonprofit pledges $40 million to help rebuild flood victims' homes," **Texas Tribune's**Colleen DeGuzman** \-- "A Hill Country nonprofit announced Thursday that it's pledging $40 million to help rebuild homes in Kerr County, the grief-stricken community hit the hardest by the July 4 weekend flash floods that devastated neighborhoods, summer camps and RV parks.

The Community Foundation of the Texas Hill Country has pledged the amount as the first step to its four-pronged recovery plan: rebuilding homes, supporting mental health, economic recovery, and reviving the area's quality of life.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/7035a9b0-48b1-46eb-a3c2-12a5bf87bc71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Community Foundation CEO **Austin Dickson** said the organization is starting with housing efforts "because rebuilding a safe, stable place to call home is at the heart of recovery .... This is more than bricks and lumber.

"Whether it's a rebuilt house, a repaired RV or a safe temporary apartment, this fund will help families get home," he later added.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/7035a9b0-48b1-46eb-a3c2-12a5bf87bc71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Dickson spoke alongside Gov. **Greg Abbott** and Hill Country leaders from a park in Kerrville -- most of the 137 Central Texas flood victims died in Kerr County. The park is about a 30-minute drive from Camp Mystic, where 27 campers and counselors died in the flood that struck in the early morning hours on July 4.

Tightening camp safety and securing relief funding are among the highest priorities of state lawmakers this special legislative session, Abbott said.

In Austin, the Texas House on Thursday passed six bills related to flood and disaster relief, including [a proposal](https://substack.com/redirect/5694206e-48c9-4e7c-8653-f3f86f1cef3a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to set aside hundreds of millions of state dollars to fund recovery efforts.

While lawmakers are discussing flood relief and response at a statewide level, Dickson said local leaders are doing what they can to jump start the recovery process. The Community Foundation on Thursday also announced that it has created a local advisory council made up of community members who will "guide funding decisions." It didn't release the names of the members Thursday.

Hunt ISD Superintendent **Luci Harmon** said rebuilding homes will help students, whose first day of the school year was Wednesday, get back to some sense of normalcy.

"Our students thrive when their housing situation is under control -- when they're in their homes and their homes are stable," she said.

The Community Foundation has also allocated $1.6 million to covering short-term housing for flood victims through AirBnB's nonprofit, [AirBnB.org](https://substack.com/redirect/da8a75d6-c5aa-4d06-b8e0-35fda0e108f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), so families have a place to stay while their houses get rebuilt." [Texas Tribune](https://substack.com/redirect/09089ef0-00c5-4e31-9b14-24644e19b239?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

TOP NEWS " Hill Country nonprofit pledges $40 million to help rebuild flood victims' homes," Texas Tribune'sColleen DeGuzman \-- "A Hill Country nonprofit announced Thursday that it's pledging $40 million to help rebuild homes in Kerr County, the grief-stricken community hit the hardest by the July 4 weekend flash floods that devastated neighborhoods, summer camps and RV parks. The Community Foundation of the Texas Hill Country has pledged the amount as the first step to its four-pronged recovery plan: rebuilding homes, supporting mental health, economic recovery, and reviving the area's quality of life. Community Foundation CEO Austin Dickson said the organization is starting with housing efforts "because rebuilding a safe, stable place to call home is at the heart of recovery .... This is more than bricks and lumber. "Whether it's a rebuilt house, a repaired RV or a safe temporary apartment, this fund will help families get home," he later added. Dickson spoke alongside Gov. Greg Abbott and Hill Country leaders from a park in Kerrville -- most of the 137 Central Texas flood victims died in Kerr County. The park is about a 30-minute drive from Camp Mystic, where 27 campers and counselors died in the flood that struck in the early morning hours on July 4. Tightening camp safety and securing relief funding are among the highest priorities of state lawmakers this special legislative session, Abbott said. In Austin, the Texas House on Thursday passed six bills related to flood and disaster relief, including to set aside hundreds of millions of state dollars to fund recovery efforts. While lawmakers are discussing flood relief and response at a statewide level, Dickson said local leaders are doing what they can to jump start the recovery process. The Community Foundation on Thursday also announced that it has created a local advisory council made up of community members who will "guide funding decisions." It didn't release the names of the members Thursday. Hunt ISD Superintendent Luci Harmon said rebuilding homes will help students, whose first day of the school year was Wednesday, get back to some sense of normalcy. "Our students thrive when their housing situation is under control -- when they're in their homes and their homes are stable," she said. The Community Foundation has also allocated $1.6 million to covering short-term housing for flood victims through AirBnB's nonprofit, , so families have a place to stay while their houses get rebuilt." Hunt ISD Superintendent **Luci Harmon** said rebuilding homes will help students, whose first day of the school year was Wednesday, get back to some sense of normalcy.

“Our students thrive when their housing situation is under control — when they're in their homes and their homes are stable,” she said.

The Community Foundation has also allocated $1.6 million to covering short-term housing for flood victims through AirBnB’s nonprofit, [AirBnB.org](https://link.mail.beehiiv.com/ss/c/u001.U4pxMavymG3FFelUcyMblQJnEQ3_Bd0TQR0dXnc_ALfTuyXg8EuOfhbGL11hbif_yCn2yor3JyYWJk7N77R1rB8lYpGQA07RwrSg5a0PGybVYV39_I92yhVGjCR29MWs6iiGx-T_pyjy6dWa9NHtmKcuiztmAFx5fIr1lhrq1xDFWQ4L7oviQpGpPTqe1hFlQjF8OdvaoenhhTuHw4sjlAqm2B_3hE4faa9D_jCklsiW1VIv1MDPfNAnbtZZOAm6hzsrgBrGcz_q3uU5jitt53fhFpSIow_5KlUGQEZw17VUtIVtVfVXCQC1yYhPrOcLvJY1xSoIIFM6ckUvkZKk9ql1Gank9XCS3Gy_MlRzMR4YrA51xzIQ_IbDbaZlkOy3/4j9/QJ9PPouoSm2I0NXJ7FBeVQ/h12/h001.g27zknlNP51lFxSGrHSbrBUfaBmxTvTw38RYKrR9oWc), so families have a place to stay while their houses get rebuilt."
