name: Keith Ellison
subject: What the Minnesota State Fair means to me
disclaimer_text: paid for by the Keith Ellison for Attorney General committee, PO Box 17224, Minneapolis, MN 55417 Keith Ellison for Attorney General
body:
The Great Minnesota Get Together reminds us that Minnesota is at its best when every voice is heard and every person belongs. | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113821612/565042506/1412364588?refcode=header&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS85MDI1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTRjMDdiMi1hNTdmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fNHpbYZi2627LVV2mtdRHP2Fcz1YKvAW2apjjWgPUO8=)
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113821614/565042509/-327406771?refcode=body&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS85MDI1MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJkNTRjMDdiMi1hNTdmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=fNHpbYZi2627LVV2mtdRHP2Fcz1YKvAW2apjjWgPUO8=)
Peter,
I’ve always said that everybody counts and everybody matters — and nowhere is that more true than at the State Fair.
At the fair, we meet people where they’re at. Our differences fade when we’re waiting in line for an annual indulgence (like an entire bucket of Sweet Martha’s cookies), enjoying a concert, or just taking it all in. No matter who you are or where you come from, the Fair has a place for you.
The Great Minnesota Get Together reminds us that Minnesota is at its best when every voice is heard and every person belongs.
That spirit of inclusion and community is what I strive for every day as the Peoples’ Lawyer, but I can’t do it alone. To keep building a Minnesota where everybody counts and everybody matters, I need your help.
Our records show that you’re still eligible for Minnesota’s Political Contribution Refund (PCR) program. That means you can donate $75 (or $150 for couples) to our campaign and receive a full refund.

clean_body: The Great Minnesota Get Together reminds us that Minnesota is at its best when every voice is heard and every person belongs. | = = Peter, I’ve always said that everybody counts and everybody matters — and nowhere is that more true than at the State Fair. At the fair, we meet people where they’re at. Our differences fade when we’re waiting in line for an annual indulgence (like an entire bucket of Sweet Martha’s cookies), enjoying a concert, or just taking it all in. No matter who you are or where you come from, the Fair has a place for you. The Great Minnesota Get Together reminds us that Minnesota is at its best when every voice is heard and every person belongs. That spirit of inclusion and community is what I strive for every day as the Peoples’ Lawyer, but I can’t do it alone. To keep building a Minnesota where everybody counts and everybody matters, I need your help. Our records show that you’re still eligible for Minnesota’s Political Contribution Refund (PCR) program. U.S. Senator Katie Britt is contributing $10,000, the maximum amount allowable, from her Alabama First leadership PAC to State Rep. Rhett Marques’ (R-Enterprise) congressional campaign.

“Rhett is a great friend and a strong conservative fighter who has always had my back and President Trump’s,” Senator Britt (R-Montgomery) said in a statement to Yellowhammer News on Thursday.

“Having been born and raised in Baldwin County and then having raised his own family in the Wiregrass, Rhett is uniquely positioned to champion the priorities and values of this incredible district. I am excited about the fresh leadership Rhett is bringing to the table and congratulate him on launching his campaign for Congress. I have no doubt that the First Congressional District will thrive with Rhett at the helm.”

U.S. Sen. Katie Britt is among those supporters of the Fiscal Year 2026 (FY26) Department of Defense (DODD) Appropriations Act, which would see more than $851 billion in discretionary spending for America’s defense capabilities. The bill recently passed through the Senate Committee on Appropriations, which Britt is a member of. Among the program increases that Britt was in support of, many include investments in Alabama’s defense capabilities. The increases will see $923 million for the Missile Defense Agency (MDA) to procure additional Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) interceptors and facilitation. The THAAD interceptors are manufactured at Lockheed Martin in Troy.

Katie Britt visited the University of Alabama at Birmingham Thursday to discuss the future of medical research.
The visit came after Britt announced advancing critical funding for Alabama medical centers, hospitals and universities. During the presentation, she discussed what the general outlook was on how Congress would approach NIH funding in the upcoming federal budget negotiations. Earlier this year, the Trump administration discussed cuts to the group as part of an effort to cut federal spending.

I didn’t run for Senate to sit on the sidelines. I’m a mom, a conservative, and proud to stand for every corner of Alabama—our cities, small towns, and rural communities alike. **I’m rooted in faith, built on family, and committed to making Alabama stronger and better for generations to come.** Alabama is a place of hard work, strong values, and unyielding independence. But the Left is trying to tear that all down. I didn’t come to Washington to play games. I came to fight for every Alabamian who feels forgotten and left behind. Whether it’s securing our borders, protecting our children, or defending our way of life, I’m here fighting for Alabama’s future. **This fight isn’t easy, but it's worth it. And I’m not going anywhere.** Will you stand with me?

clean_body: | | | | | | | U.S. Senator Katie Britt is contributing $10,000, the maximum amount allowable, from her Alabama First leadership PAC to State Rep. Rhett Marques’ (R-Enterprise) congressional campaign. “Rhett is a great friend and a strong conservative fighter who has always had my back and President Trump’s,” Senator Britt (R-Montgomery) said in a statement to Yellowhammer News on Thursday. “Having been born and raised in Baldwin County and then having raised his own family in the Wiregrass, Rhett is uniquely positioned to champion the priorities and values of this incredible district. I am excited about the fresh leadership Rhett is bringing to the table and congratulate him on launching his campaign for Congress. I have no doubt that the First Congressional District will thrive with Rhett at the helm.” | | U.S. Sen. Katie Britt is among those supporters of the Fiscal Year 2026 (FY26) Department of Defense (DODD) Appropriations Act, which would see more than $851 billion in discretionary spending for America’s defense capabilities. The bill recently passed through the Senate Committee on Appropriations, which Britt is a member of. Among the program increases that Britt was in support of, many include investments in Alabama’s defense capabilities. The increases will see $923 million for the Missile Defense Agency (MDA) to procure additional Terminal High Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) interceptors and facilitation. The THAAD interceptors are manufactured at Lockheed Martin in Troy. | | BIRMINGHAM, Ala. () — National Institute of Health Director Dr. Jay Bhattacharya and Sen. Katie Britt visited the University of Alabama at Birmingham Thursday to discuss the future of medical research. The visit came after Britt announced advancing critical funding for Alabama medical centers, hospitals and universities. During the presentation, she discussed what the general outlook was on how Congress would approach NIH funding in the upcoming federal budget negotiations. Earlier this year, the Trump administration discussed cuts to the group as part of an effort to cut federal spending. | | | | | | | I didn’t run for Senate to sit on the sidelines. name: Jon Ossoff
subject: Respectfully asking
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Chris Pappas for Senate
body:
We flipped Georgia when no one thought it was possible.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_20250822_2_ossoff?refcode=em20250822-1974&amount=5&refcode2=1974_582294_jfe3io&akid=1974%2E582294%2Ejfe3io)
Hey there, it’s Jon. I’m respectfully asking: [**Can you rush $5 to my reelection campaign and Chris Pappas today?**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_20250822_2_ossoff?refcode=em20250822-1974&amount=5&refcode2=1974_582294_jfe3io&akid=1974%2E582294%2Ejfe3io)
Sorry to be so direct, but winning in 2026 is our _only_ hope to regain Congressional power and reverse our ongoing descent toward one-man rule.
And I am the _only_ Democratic U.S. Senator up for reelection in a state Trump won.
That makes me MAGA’s #1 target in 2026. They’re _already_ spending MILLIONS of dollars to try to defeat me.
**_There is no path back from the brink of authoritarianism without defending this seat — the very seat that secured the Democratic majority in 2021._**
So I'm asking for your help. Not for me, **for all of us.**
[**If you’re able, please make a contribution of any amount to my campaign and Chris Pappas today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_20250822_2_ossoff?refcode=em20250822-1974&amount=5&refcode2=1974_582294_jfe3io&akid=1974%2E582294%2Ejfe3io)
The campaign will be brutal.
_But this team wins the hardest battles._
We flipped Georgia when no one thought it was possible.
Remember? We unseated an entrenched GOP incumbent to win the Senate majority.
And we will win again — with your help.
[**Please: send $5 to my reelection campaign and Chris Pappas today.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_20250822_2_ossoff?refcode=em20250822-1974&amount=5&refcode2=1974_582294_jfe3io&akid=1974%2E582294%2Ejfe3io)
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_cp_20250822_2_ossoff?amount=5&refcode=em20250822-1974&refcode2=1974_582294_jfe3io&akid=1974%2E582294%2Ejfe3io)**
With gratitude,
Senator Jon Ossoff
**New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?**

clean_body: We flipped Georgia when no one thought it was possible. Hey there, it’s Jon. I’m respectfully asking: Sorry to be so direct, but winning in 2026 is our only hope to regain Congressional power and reverse our ongoing descent toward one-man rule. And I am the only Democratic U.S. Senator up for reelection in a state Trump won. That makes me MAGA’s #1 target in 2026. They’re already spending MILLIONS of dollars to try to defeat me. There is no path back from the brink of authoritarianism without defending this seat — the very seat that secured the Democratic majority in 2021. So I'm asking for your help. Not for me, for all of us. The campaign will be brutal. But this team wins the hardest battles. We flipped Georgia when no one thought it was possible. Remember? We unseated an entrenched GOP incumbent to win the Senate majority. And we will win again — with your help. With gratitude, Senator Jon Ossoff New Hampshire is poised to be one of the closest Senate races in the country in 2026. Will you chip in today to help Chris stop the Republicans from flipping the Granite State red and expanding their Senate majority?

name: Roy Cooper
subject: They’re going to make the climate crisis worse.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
If we’re not willing to protect the planet, nothing else we do matters. |
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113822108/565044137/823540626?refcode=ea.5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MzY5ZTllYi1hOTdmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UNoyy66VPtSd0MWfxPr6ZSWhjC3z9d6XGbiRn9qlrjU=&emci=7f0a6fd7-617f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=17205535)
**Republicans are not only ignoring the threat of climate change, Peter. They’re actively making it worse.** The Trump Administration is planning to rescind a longstanding EPA scientific directive declaring that greenhouse gases endanger public health and welfare. That directive is the basis for safeguards that have helped keep our air and water clean and more investments in clean energy which have been creating jobs in rural communities and making our country more globally competitive with EVs and renewable energy. **[Our elected leaders should share a commitment to protecting our planet — if we can’t promise future generations a liveable world, nothing else we do matters. This reckless action by the federal government is misguided at best and malicious at worst, and we can’t allow them to get away with it. Add your name to my petition calling on the Trump Administration to uphold the EPA rule >>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113822110/565044139/1777067028?refcode=ea.5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MzY5ZTllYi1hOTdmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UNoyy66VPtSd0MWfxPr6ZSWhjC3z9d6XGbiRn9qlrjU=&emci=7f0a6fd7-617f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=17205535)** Greenhouse gases are a main driver of climate change. The science is clear, and it shouldn’t be a matter up for political debate. National Republicans claim they’re taking this step because taking climate change seriously could “bankrupt the country.” But that’s just not true. During my time as Governor of North Carolina, we prioritized clean energy initiatives — and rather than harming our economy, **our investment in the clean energy sector brought new businesses and good-paying jobs to our state.** That’s the kind of progress we need to see on a national scale, Peter, and I’m committed to helping create it as the next U.S. Senator from North Carolina. It starts by upholding the EPA rule and recognizing the truth that we all know: greenhouse gases cause climate change, and climate change is an existential threat to life as we know it. **[I need you to join me and speak out against the Trump Administration’s dangerous anti-climate action. Sign on here to call on the federal government to leave the EPA rule in place >>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113822113/565044142/1777067028?refcode=ea.5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MzY5ZTllYi1hOTdmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UNoyy66VPtSd0MWfxPr6ZSWhjC3z9d6XGbiRn9qlrjU=&emci=7f0a6fd7-617f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=17205535)** | [SIGN ON](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113822114/565044144/1777067028?refcode=ea.5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ms=ea.5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODE3NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MzY5ZTllYi1hOTdmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=UNoyy66VPtSd0MWfxPr6ZSWhjC3z9d6XGbiRn9qlrjU=&emci=7f0a6fd7-617f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&emdi=5369e9eb-a97f-f011-b484-6045bdeb7413&ceid=17205535)

Thanks for speaking out,
Roy Cooper

Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

clean_body: If we’re not willing to protect the planet, nothing else we do matters. | Republicans are not only ignoring the threat of climate change, Peter. They’re actively making it worse. The Trump Administration is planning to rescind a longstanding EPA scientific directive declaring that greenhouse gases endanger public health and welfare. That directive is the basis for safeguards that have helped keep our air and water clean and more investments in clean energy which have been creating jobs in rural communities and making our country more globally competitive with EVs and renewable energy. Greenhouse gases are a main driver of climate change. The science is clear, and it shouldn’t be a matter up for political debate. National Republicans claim they’re taking this step because taking climate change seriously could “bankrupt the country.” But that’s just not true. During my time as Governor of North Carolina, we prioritized clean energy initiatives — and rather than harming our economy, our investment in the clean energy sector brought new businesses and good-paying jobs to our state. That’s the kind of progress we need to see on a national scale, Peter, and I’m committed to helping create it as the next U.S. Senator from North Carolina. It starts by upholding the EPA rule and recognizing the truth that we all know: greenhouse gases cause climate change, and climate change is an existential threat to life as we know it. | Thanks for speaking out, Roy Cooper Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here. National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us. Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party. Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy. = = = mailto:info@roycooper.com | Cooper for North Carolina PO Box 1190 Raleigh, NC 27602 Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina Contributions or gifts to Cooper for North Carolina are not tax deductible. ### _Democratic Party Identity Part I (of IV): Be Sherrod Brown_
Since the 2024 election mishegas, polls have increasingly shown the Democratic brand to be on par with hantavirus.
This even as the opposition's led by a cankled cream puff who wines like a railroad engine and possesses the personal taste of a Saddam-Hussein palace dry humping Liberace. A gauche, diapered [ignoramus who's crashing the economy and increasingly has gravy thickener for brains](https://substack.com/redirect/86d2c52c-884d-4178-8bc0-a9d88d11e083?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
And we're not talking about Cornaby's E-Z Gel here, but the generic shit.
Now there are many reasons for Democrats' foibles, some of which we'll talk about in the other parts of this series.
They include Democrats' lack of a messaging machine anywhere close to the leviathan Republicans have built. The lack of cojones that would instruct Dems _not to_ _just do the right thing,_ even when it enables fascism.
And a basic unwillingness to dump traditions older than Chuck Grassley's liver spots that appear nowhere in the Constitution--see debt ceiling, filibuster, blue slips--yet are readily abused and/or ignored by Republicans.
But there is something else just as important--and that is the candidate. You've likely heard the saying the medium is the message. In politics, also, the messenger is the message.
If you ever wondered how [Roy Cooper has won so solidly ](https://substack.com/redirect/b0e5fc2d-e776-4fd9-9fb4-5a2bfc3a16e5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[in North Carolina](https://substack.com/redirect/6b9c2cc8-2b63-46b0-9d98-085bab22cd2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), Laura Kelly is Governor of Kansas and Andy Beshear is Governor of Kentucky, just to name a few, _it 's because candidates really do matter._
And that is where one of the best I've ever seen, and the one who can show Democrats the way, comes in: Sherrod Brown.
As Democrats search for [identity, culture and the the right policies that reinforce the values that the first two communicate](https://substack.com/redirect/d86dd96e-ee1f-4eb1-b75c-af1efbcfca74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to voters, Democrats must lean into the roadmap Sherrod provided for years to win handily in Ohio. Even as most Democrats lost the ability to appeal to many of of Ohio's voters outside of the 3 Cs (Cincinnati, Columbus, Cleveland).
Look, even ignoring the dingos of the right-wing echoes-phere and [corporate media click bait ladies of the night](https://substack.com/redirect/899e7775-c38c-4362-a961-5b2b089324c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), isn't to say there's not a problem here. Because there is, it's a major one and it's one some of us have been screaming about for years.
[From the days of FDR, Democrats were the party of the working class](https://substack.com/redirect/bb5f7753-cf07-4b4a-85c9-bfb711fc2cdb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). That came to include not just the white guys with a hard hat and a hammer corporate media equates with that term, but the Black, Hispanic and Asian working class too.
Yet, we not only further hemorrhaged white working class and middle class voters this last election [to a freakish, self-loathing](https://substack.com/redirect/76e7881d-6e9a-45bc-ae18-b6502a48a34d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), diarrhea-of-the-mouth dingus. But our problem became multi-racial.
We lost many working-class-minority men to a human oil spot who began his campaign by calling Mexicans rapists, referred to nations in Africa as "shithole countries" and called COVID the "Kung-Flu."
Scranton, we have a problem.
And the solution is Sherrod Brown.
Brown is exactly the kind of scrappy, salt-of-the-Earth Democrat we need out front speaking for the party right now. Is he a little older than you might want? In a perfect world, yes. But we live in this particular very-non-perfect world.
And in this one, Sherrod is the goods.
He's always been ahead of the curve, even back when Ohio swung back and forth almost equally. In other words, before greedy, democracy-hating, perpetually papyrus-brained Republicans turned my home state into MAGA LEGOLAND. Sherrod's always been a populist, workers-first kinda Democrat.
Probably why I feel so comfortable calling him Sherrod, even though we've only met a few times.
And the thing is Sherrod isn't just populist--every single quality of his screams it.
From a gravelly voice that makes you picture a guy who chain-smoked a pack of Marlboro Reds while simultaneously chugging black coffees by the dozen, to the oft unkempt curls in his hair and regular dude look he has on the campaign trail.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/113e7d7c-1217-4270-bf11-b77722c4e7b7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
#### _**It 's an honor to share our knowledge, experience & ideas with you. We don't say enough how much we appreciate you! Yet, this is also a [reader-supported effort](https://substack.com/redirect/899e7775-c38c-4362-a961-5b2b089324c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which can't continue at this pace without your support. So, please, seriously consider a [Paid Subscription](https://substack.com/redirect/899e7775-c38c-4362-a961-5b2b089324c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**_
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.KdSeRkbKovPIbMqpMtMW-6EjgkTfAkqaXFVTbsGpX9o?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget&utm_content=171482518)
From the union halls he still packs to the little yellow canary pin he wears and speaks about often, given to him by a miner, reminds him of whom he fights for and makes clear where his true allegiance lies.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/56ac4f9a-4e5e-4a4d-831d-295e0c93afa0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Sherrod was talking about the unfair trade deals screwing workers while Donald Trump was still screwing porn stars. Brown was calling for Democrats to fight for stronger unions. He even talked tariffs--not the "wake up on the wrong side of the un-man cave and [slap tariffs on everything in sight](https://substack.com/redirect/301ee9ee-9dc2-4b9d-a813-eda6ebd73678?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" dill-holery of Trump.
But targeted, strategic, narrow ones aimed right at countries unfairly dumping products here to harm American workers. That's Sherrod--smart populism, not [dumb as a doorknob policies](https://substack.com/redirect/a84457e5-8294-401b-bb4e-2a5af698b89f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
This doesn't mean that Sherrod doesn't support, and fight for, a woman's right to control her body. He does. Or our freedom to send our kids to school and not have them massacred by some hallucinating, MAGA jackwad who hates the world.
But he always--_always_ --leads with worker pay, healthcare for all, family & medical leave, the right to organize, safety on the job and more. He talks about the "dignity of work" constantly.
These aren't just themes he trots out when he is speaking to workers, they are themes of who he is. His moral center.
[He lives and breathes these values.](https://substack.com/redirect/64ac8cea-08ea-4fd6-a5d2-f0c3fd2fb21b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
This is what makes him alway seem like an outsider, because he doesn't talk in the shallow sentences of Washington. Sadly, neither does Trump, which is what has made him popular with some of these same crowds.
It's just that [Trump is a serial liar that is cruel, corrupt and incompetent in reality](https://substack.com/redirect/f9444e23-e3d5-494e-89b7-6503aa5d98ae?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
You need the cred Sherrod brings to beat the MAGA movement. Joe Biden definitely embodied some of this in 2020 with his faith out front, his stories, gaffes and constant talk about Scranton and being a regular guy. He also lived it. But Sherrod is on a whole different level.
And we're in the age of the non-politician, politician. [People want conversations, not dictation](https://substack.com/redirect/d86dd96e-ee1f-4eb1-b75c-af1efbcfca74?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). They want authenticity. They want real.
Trump is real. A real piece of shit, but real.
Sherrod is the right kind of real. The kind that met my family, including our then two-year old, and sent us a letter a week later[ addressed to ](https://substack.com/redirect/d3a56959-a533-41ba-b15c-78efd9a2dbe6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)[him](https://substack.com/redirect/bd460c8d-5788-4d1a-a047-5d30f83e3366?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It was about how much he enjoyed meeting him and [how smart my young son was to bring his parents with him](https://substack.com/redirect/bd460c8d-5788-4d1a-a047-5d30f83e3366?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to the event.
It's this that for the better part of 50 years in electoral politics in Ohio, allowed him not just survive, but thrive. He's as comfortable at a big-check fundraiser as he is at a trucker hall in Toledo. He's one of the only Democrats who can walk into both rooms and sound like the same damn guy!
No double-speak, no "different script for different audiences" bullshit. Just Sherrod.
Yes, as you're going to say, he lost his race in 2024. But did you know he only lost by 3%--**over-performed the top of the Democratic ticket by 8%**.
That was when Trump was on the ballot, semi-popular--i.e. not hated by many of his own voters as he is now--and Brown's opponent, Bernie Moreno, also came off like an outsider. Being a gauche, newly-rich, loudmouth-of-a car salesman who sounded just enough like an asshole--because he is one--to be Trump will do that for you. Also, he hadn't held public office.
This time Sherrod is running against Jon Husted. LOL. An insider's insider. A guy who looks like he was born in khakis and docksiders and his parents registed him as a lobbyist as a fetus.
And Sherrod's template is one that other authentic candidates can adopt.
[Hallie Shofner, running for Senate in Arkansas, a 6th generation farmer](https://substack.com/redirect/cddf933a-e368-4b8f-8b37-c417dd61bc2d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who lost her farm to Trump's Tuberville-esque tariffs and is now taking on Tom Cotton, showed exactly this kind of politics. She attacked the Democratic Establishment in DC for abandoning Arkansas, right before launching into a tirade about how Cotton had betrayed Arkansans like her, voting against the Farm Bill TWICE.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYmx1ZWFtcC5jby9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.KdSeRkbKovPIbMqpMtMW-6EjgkTfAkqaXFVTbsGpX9o?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=171482518)
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/6d7ab678-5fe3-4b89-8628-94f84732830f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
A real farmer, like another authentic Senator who way over-performed the top of the ticket in 2024--by 12% actually--former Senator Jon Tester in Montana. Had that race been somewhat closer at the top he could've won too.
To get back to Sherrod, as I said, he is the model. [Liberal on social issues](https://substack.com/redirect/3a0b5adc-c08a-49fb-97c8-093619c28fbc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) but doesn't lead with them (and at this point can easily frame them as freedom against the peeping Tom, pedo-enablers).
He leads with the issues that have the broadest appeal to our emerging coalition and which formed the basis of Democratic dominance for half the 20th Century. Sherrod leads with work. With dignity. With the core idea that no matter who you are, you deserve a fair shot.
That's why he can bring back working-class Latinos, Black voters, Asian voters, younger voters **and yes, win more men in general**. While continuing to win big with women in the suburbs and other minority voters, and pull in chunks of MAGA-lite working-class whites who went for Trump.
**That 's the coalition we need to [rebuild the Democratic Party into something like FDR's New Deal coalition](https://substack.com/redirect/24bfdf36-80b0-4baa-bda8-e7353328a0ed?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**
The messaging isn't hard and it's already in Sherrod's toolbox and should be repeated constantly by all Democrats.
As even wealthy-by-all-standards Republicans and Independents are left to look at crazy interest rates, inflation and a declining standard of living where they can't afford college or childcare and worry their healthcare won't cover an important operation.
They now wonder why if they're making hundreds of thousands of dollars a year, they feel like they're barely treading water. Sherrod--and the rest of us--can tell them. [The message is literally sitting right there](https://substack.com/redirect/6b9c2cc8-2b63-46b0-9d98-085bab22cd2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--and it's the truth!
They're like the working class, at least in terms of how Trump, Musk and Thiel--The 3 Stool-ges--see them.
Yes, you can make a few hundred grand, but if you're paying for a creepy, socially-Gary-Busey'd, robo-spawning-loser like Elon Musk to slurp up government contracts and mold monopolies that raise all prices, cut your benefits, squeeze you out of housing with their private equity acquisitions, etc--it doesn't much matter that you've done well by most standards.
You're not an oligarch. And unfortunately for you, person who'd normally be rich, [we're living in an oligarchy](https://substack.com/redirect/dd8aaa66-d3b0-42f5-b3b9-3696c662cf9a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Sherrod speaks to all of the above. To the idea that you should be able to work hard and still afford a good life, in fact a better life than your parents--which was once considered the American Dream.
Murdered by the South African tech bros and a gold-fondling, Queens colostomy bag. Who when you say "let's get down to business," immediately bankrupts the nearest animate object.
Sure, [Republicans may shoot their puppies in the face, protect the files of a dead, international super-predator](https://substack.com/redirect/1a3e1658-590b-4bf4-a510-116a0fdaadcb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--**RELEASE THE EPSTEIN FILES, NO WE HAVEN 'T FORGOTTEN, DONNY**--and take off on a first class jaunts every time their constituents are freezing or flooded to death.
But they'll still call Democrats elitists and much of [the corporate media](https://substack.com/redirect/899e7775-c38c-4362-a961-5b2b089324c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) will let them get away with it.
Right Jake Tapper?!?
That's why **Sherrod Brown is the guy Democrats need to look to for messaging and cultural identity** right now. An authentic populist who knows how to unite the left and center, who knows the stakes and never forgot for whom he fights.
If Democrats want to build a coalition that lasts another 50 years, they should stop looking to poll-tested consultants and listening to cable-media pundit slop, and look to Sherrod Brown.
Because if anyone can realign the party with real people, the vast majority of people in America, it's Sherrod.

body:
As we approach the start of early voting, I’ve been reflecting on why I decided to run for Governor.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113822310/565044604/830632682?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-08-22_2&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYmZjNmNiNTAtYWI3Zi1mMDExLWI0ODQtNjA0NWJkZWI3NDEzIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=hf4t2a2dJDIdIp8hrR8V1Zc49iHv4dWcxHu5KOBhIHY=)
Peter,
As we approach the start of early voting, I’ve been reflecting on why I decided to run for Governor.
It wasn’t for a title or to check a box. _I got into this race because I know how much better Virginia can be — for our kids, our neighbors, and our communities._
From my time serving Virginia’s Seventh District in Congress, I’ve seen firsthand how much good we can do when we come together to solve problems and put people over politics.
**That’s the kind of leadership I believe in — and it’s the kind I’ll bring to the Governor’s office.**
With the chaos and division coming out of Washington, Virginia deserves steady, thoughtful leadership more than ever. Leadership that puts Virginians first, that protects our rights, and that focuses on all of the issues that matter to families across the Commonwealth.
_But with time running out to meet our August fundraising goal, I’m counting on your support to keep us on track._
[_**Please consider making a contribution to my campaign before midnight. clean_body: As we approach the start of early voting, I’ve been reflecting on why I decided to run for Governor. = Peter, As we approach the start of early voting, I’ve been reflecting on why I decided to run for Governor. It wasn’t for a title or to check a box. I got into this race because I know how much better Virginia can be — for our kids, our neighbors, and our communities. From my time serving Virginia’s Seventh District in Congress, I’ve seen firsthand how much good we can do when we come together to solve problems and put people over politics. That’s the kind of leadership I believe in — and it’s the kind I’ll bring to the Governor’s office. With the chaos and division coming out of Washington, Virginia deserves steady, thoughtful leadership more than ever. Leadership that puts Virginians first, that protects our rights, and that focuses on all of the issues that matter to families across the Commonwealth. But with time running out to meet our August fundraising goal, I’m counting on your support to keep us on track. = = = = = = = Thank you, Abigail Spanberger By submitting your phone number you're agreeing to receive periodic text messages from the Spanberger for Governor campaign. name: Mark Harris
subject: Standing Strong for Our Families - Will You Stand with Me?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mark Harris for Congress
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/markharrisforcongress.com/standing-strong-for-our-families-will-you-stand-with-me?e=4024c5123e)
---
| | [ ](https://markharrisforcongress.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=82117f51bb8b79bb18d514427&id=19c6f67723&e=4024c5123e)
---
| **Dear Friend,**
I'm reaching out with a heart full of gratitude for your steadfast support. **For years, as a pastor, I’ve been blessed to guide our community through faith, and now, as your representative for North Carolina’s 8th District, I’m fighting with that same conviction to uplift our working families and honor the values we hold dear.** I’m thrilled to share the good news of the One Big Beautiful Bill Act, a victory we secured alongside the Freedom Caucus. This bill is a blessing for our hardworking folks:
* **No tax on tips, ensuring our servers, drivers, and service workers keep their hard-earned wages.**
* **No federal tax on overtime pay, honoring those who labor extra hours to provide for their loved ones.**
* **$7,200 to $12,200 more in the pockets of a typical family of four, with wages across North Carolina poised to rise by $5,500 to $10,500 a year in time.**

This bill reflects our shared belief in rewarding honest work, easing burdens, and keeping Washington’s hands off what you’ve earned. It’s a testament to what we can do when we stand firm for our values and put our people first. **👇 CLICK TO READ NOW 👇**
---
| [ ](https://markharrisforcongress.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=82117f51bb8b79bb18d514427&id=1c832d4f72&e=4024c5123e)
---
| **But the work continues, and the 2026 midterms are drawing near.** To keep fighting for secure borders, less government waste, and policies that reflect our faith and principles, I humbly ask for your support. Your generosity will help us carry this message across the 8th District:
* **A gift of $25 shares our vision with 100 more voters.**
* **A gift of $50 spreads the word through campaign materials.**
* **A gift of $100 or more strengthens our efforts to rally our community for the battles ahead.**

---
| [DONATE NOW!](https://markharrisforcongress.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=82117f51bb8b79bb18d514427&id=61d29ee00e&e=4024c5123e "DONATE NOW!")
---
| _Thank you for standing with us._

Sincerely,

Mark Harris

name: Gabe Vasquez
subject: This is not how we should treat people
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gabe Vasquez for Congress
body:
Gabe Vasquez won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022. He's running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/lNYIdkhPikOBrh6RGc-KWg2?unsubscribedata=e3ogLx0gbat9WU3aexqgfSegXvVURY2e0dNAy1TJZhspsnKnNqITLsltKQCd%2FeQ4I1PLTtC5ZEOen9LCR6jgUp4621z0pLHuNCSqVSJ5E0%2B8hO0rZQGZPYya2Ri0jGCDvRa9KoE%2FAR4mJnvKGKaYQp8PqQTOvUsX8EDjHz0j5eT1w6hcPgcc%2BZlMJg7MjscW6vJNRdNIvHDryf5%2FJIjtHVeeTz5f%2Fq5sUhI7COCiA3nvBx3AQNYqNvCWMJIbGJ0XO7EnQxHmOWzyndCzg3v5tzO3q1KwFJgbzLvS%2FG0nThQElXrV6awTGhx0qkgzolpuiR%2BtHF8WBrmo8ETJf67TT4FhBi9Y78j1zCQOMxx9qugELNhwzmEQKjJkC56Gq1KPtbVghUHLvzhLelm09t3%2BVeK9tEMqRHiXsSpXAzXwCQw%3D)
---
| |
---
It's Gabe. In July, Trump and the Republicans pushed through a tax law that gave billionaires a huge break — to pay for it, they slashed $180 billion from SNAP, the program that helps people buy food. It’s the biggest cut to food assistance in our country’s history — and here in New Mexico, it could mean 175,000 people lose the help they count on to put food on the table. We’re talking about families where the grocery budget is already stretched thin. Seniors who rely on SNAP and Meals on Wheels to get by. Kids in early childhood programs who may not have anything to eat at home — except maybe a bowl of cereal, if that. **The idea that food is where we’re going to “tighten the belt” — while billionaires cash in — is flat-out backwards.** We should be doing the opposite — _expanding access, not shrinking it._ We should be making sure no kid goes to bed hungry. We should be making sure our parents and grandparents are taken care of. We should be making sure that no one in southern New Mexico — or anywhere — has to wonder where their next meal is coming from. [That’s what I’m fighting for. But I need your help to keep going. Will you chip in $10 or whatever you can to help us keep up the fight — and make sure southern New Mexico’s families don’t get left behind?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/113823038/565047047/-2084511163?amount=10&source=SBD_GV_EM_FR_082225_FOODINS_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85MzAxMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxYjcxYzY4Yy1hYzdmLWYwMTEtYjQ4NC02MDQ1YmRlYjc0MTMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=_AjZm7sFmCpTvtJ-wp6824janTTVyhZqI5MKm1M-3zc=) | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now.

clean_body: Gabe Vasquez won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022. He's running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | It's Gabe. In July, Trump and the Republicans pushed through a tax law that gave billionaires a huge break — to pay for it, they slashed $180 billion from SNAP, the program that helps people buy food. It’s the biggest cut to food assistance in our country’s history — and here in New Mexico, it could mean 175,000 people lose the help they count on to put food on the table. We’re talking about families where the grocery budget is already stretched thin. Seniors who rely on SNAP and Meals on Wheels to get by. Kids in early childhood programs who may not have anything to eat at home — except maybe a bowl of cereal, if that. The idea that food is where we’re going to “tighten the belt” — while billionaires cash in — is flat-out backwards. We should be doing the opposite — expanding access, not shrinking it. We should be making sure no kid goes to bed hungry. We should be making sure our parents and grandparents are taken care of. We should be making sure that no one in southern New Mexico — or anywhere — has to wonder where their next meal is coming from. = | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process now. | = | = | = | = | = | = Thanks for standing with us, Gabe | | | ---|---|--- = Together, we flipped NM-02 in 2022. Now, Gabe is busy in Congress fighting for reproductive choice, economic opportunity, affordable healthcare, and the best interests of the people. Gabe won the most competitive Democratic seat in Congress in 2022, and is running to be a champion for working families and build an economy that benefits everyone.** | = = Paid for by Gabe Vasquez for Congress Gabe Vasquez for Congress Drawer L Mesilla, NM 88046 United States If you would like to unsubscribe, click here: To receive less email from Gabe Vasquez for Congress, . name: Rob Bonta
subject: Protecting immigrant families
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ROB BONTA FOR CALIFORNIA ATTORNEY GENERAL 2026 FPPC ID #145642]
body:
[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/4?refcode=em250822&t=1001&akid=1922%2E1088799%2Ey917Il ]Rob Bonta

Last
night I joined California Democrats for a townhall on immigration — I want
to share some of my remarks with you because the stakes are too high to
stay silent.

I spoke about the dangerous, and at times unlawful, tactics Trump is using
to intimidate immigrant families and divide our communities — how he’s
cultivating fear, mistrust, and confusion.

How he’s bringing in military trucks. He’s sending federal agents in masks
and plainclothes into our communities. They’re driving unmarked vehicles
and jumping out of moving vans, refusing to identify themselves when
asked.

Trump’s tactics are dangerous and inhumane. His actions are damaging
community trust in law enforcement, disrupting our lives — and they are
deeply un-American.

I refuse to stand idly by while this administration terrorizes our
communities and repeatedly breaks the law.

That’s why in just 30 weeks, I’ve filed 39 lawsuits against Trump —
including:

* Blocking his attempt to end birthright citizenship
* Stopping him from tying vital federal funding to immigration
enforcement
* Challenging his illegal deployment of federal troops in Los Angeles

And at the California Department of Justice, we’re making sure people know
their rights. My office has published resources for immigrant families,
schools, and communities in multiple languages — because knowledge is
power.

Every lawsuit, every action, every resource takes support – but I can’t
keep up these fights unless I win re-election next year.

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-250822?refcode=em20250822-1922&refcode2=1922_1088799_y917Il&akid=1922%2E1088799%2Ey917Il ]Will you chip in today to my re-election campaign so I can keep
fighting back against Trump’s unlawful attacks and continue defending
California families?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rb-email-250822?refcode=em20250822-1922&amount=5&refcode2=1922_1088799_y917Il&akid=1922%2E1088799%2Ey917Il ]Donate $5 »

California is the most diverse state in the nation — and that’s our
greatest strength. I’ll never stop fighting to protect it.

Thank you,
Rob

[ https://act.robbonta.com/go/1044?t=1002&akid=1922%2E1088799%2Ey917Il ][IMG]

Rob Bonta
Attorney General of California

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:

name: The Democrats
subject: UPDATE: Trump wants to steal 100 more seats
body:
---
Derek, **Trump just said he wants to steal _100 more congressional seats_ ahead of the 2026 midterms:** [](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?refcode=em_20250822_4401_fr&amount=3&refcode2=4401_22930333_MYprN3&akid=4401%2E22930333%2EMYprN3) Republicans’ gerrymandered map in Texas was just the beginning. Already, we’re seeing his allies in other Republican-dominated states like Florida and Missouri beginning to look at redistricting their own states. In Missouri’s case, lawmakers have already called for these discussions, despite pushback from their Democratic colleagues. On top of that, Trump is tying these plans _directly_ to his push to end mail-in voting, in a direct assault on our democracy. **Here’s the truth: Republicans know they can’t win fair and square in 2026, so they’re using every tool in their belt to rig the midterms and keep themselves in power.** In California, Democrats are advancing their own redistricting plan in opposition, but the work doesn’t end there. The DNC is already suing Trump for his attacks on voting rights, and we are doubling down on expanding our organizing work to make sure folks are ready to vote like their rights depend on it — _because they do._ [**If protecting voting rights and stopping Trump’s plan to steal next year’s midterms is worth $3 to you, that’s the donation we’re asking you to make to the DNC today to help us fund this work nationwide.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?amount=3&refcode=em_20250822_4401_fr&refcode2=4401_22930333_MYprN3&akid=4401%2E22930333%2EMYprN3) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?express_lane=true&amount=3&refcode=em_20250822_4401_fr&refcode2=4401_22930333_MYprN3&akid=4401%2E22930333%2EMYprN3)**
